wheel-0001.name: Merciful Nurturing
wheel-0001.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: Hand Limit +2. At the end of your turn, gain Keyflare equal to the wielder's [StateArg1]% Keyflare Regen. After each Posse is Unleashed, all Awakeners gain [StateArg2] Aliemus. If the current Realm is {Caro}, also trigger {Embryo Fusion} +[StateArg3].
wheel-0001.lore:
History has always been a one-sided narrative of the victors; the Mother Tree perished, nations fell, and everything about the past is quietly changing.
The poet sings the tale of the Nineveh beauty, while travelers gather around the campfire to listen intently.

"Traversing the silver desert, the foreign beauty intruded upon Nineveh.
The waters of the Milky Way slipped from her shoulders, Sinai pearls fell from her eyes.
In her golden pupils, gazing at the past of another land.
Upon her honey-colored skin, the future of Nineveh is inscribed."

And then? Did this beauty become a queen?
The aged poet plucks the strings of his seven-stringed lute, releasing a long sigh.

"She is a soporific, causing all beings to fall under her spell.
She is a poison, penetrating the lungs of kings and ministers.
She is a flood, from which no one can escape.
She is the harbinger of the black plague, bringing illness, nightmares, death, and the end of Nineveh."

Before the lute is set down, a figure stands up from the table, drawing the attention of the patrons.
The slender fingers of that person let slip some coins for wine, gold and silver rolling and clinking.
Wherever she goes, gazes follow, lights flicker.

"Time is fleeting... but it matters not, He will not respond to these nonsensical words.
One day, you will understand His mercy and generosity... just as I do..."
She pushes open the worn little door of the tavern, like opening a jewel box, moonlight falling on her bare shoulders, reflecting a scintillating glow.
The beauty, basked in moonlight, indeed has skin as sweet as honey.

wheel-0002.name: Tablet of Scriptures
wheel-0002.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: The wielder's Shield Generation +[StateArg1]%, and HP Recovery +[StateArg1]%. At the end of odd turns, the wielder's Crit. Rate + [StateArg2]%, gaining [StateArg3] Aliemus; at the end of even turns, the wielder's Crit. DMG +[StateArg2]%, gaining [StateArg4] Keyflare. If the current realm is {Caro}, the maximum accumulation of the {Crimson Furnace} +[StateArg5]%.
wheel-0002.lore:
The Father of All Beginnings came from gulfs beyond the stars.

His feet trod upon the flames, and He spoke, "Let the fire fade."

The molten earth grew still, and upon its face, the Father inscribed the First Law:
"Let the land be the bedrock of existence."
Thus were the valleys cloven, and the plains unfurled vast.
And the Word was wrought in stone, the First Tablet decreed.

Then the All-Father turned His gaze unto the burdened clouds, and He proclaimed: "Let the clouds pour forth weeping."
The waters amassed, and upon them He carved the Second Law:
"Let the waters be the cradle of being."
And the waters begat lakes, and lakes swelled into seas, and the seas consumed the margins of the earth.
And the Word was wrought in stone, the Second Tablet anointed.

Then the All-Father beheld the waters, black and voiceless, and He intoned: "Let there be light."
Light burst forth, and upon it He carved the Third Law:
"Let the light sustain."
And warmth stole across the depths unplumbed.
And the Word was wrought in stone, the Third Tablet consecrated.

Then the All-Father saw earth, water, and light forged, and He declared:
"Let life awaken."
He let fall His blood, and upon it He carved the Fourth Law:
"Let the blood hasten life."
And His blood rained upon the waters, and the deep teemed with life.
And the Word was wrought in stone, the Fourth Tablet Exalted.

Then the All-Father saw life take root, and He spake:
"Let life transmute without end."
Minute beings crept upon the land where waters receded, and upon them He carved the Fifth Law:
"Change shall not slumber."
And they waxed in roots, in limbs, in forms manifold.
And the Word was wrought in stone, the Fifth Tablet sealed.

Then the All-Father saw life and change abound, and He ordained: "Let death also descend." And death arose.
The hoary and feeble faded, and upon them He carved the Sixth Law:
"The dead shall return unto the genesis."
And their flesh melted, drawn back to the All-Father's unseen fold.
And the Word was wrought in stone, the Sixth Tablet inscribed.

Then the All-Father beheld life, death, and change entwined, and He spake:
"These are My deeds, and I shall grant a boon."
He took of His own bone, and upon it He carved the Seventh Law:
"At the end of all, My bone shall hold My dominion. Let flesh be the cost, and life be rekindled."
And the Word was wrought in stone, the Seventh Tablet hallowed.

And the All-Father looked upon all He had shaped, and He reposed.
The waters enwrapped Him, the light lent Him solace, the earth veiled Him.

And the All-Father slumbered. All things endure by His Word.
So it standeth, even unto this age.

wheel-0003.name: Winter's Requiem
wheel-0003.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: At the start of combat, apply 2 stacks of {Vulnerable} to front-row enemies. At the start of each turn, enemies in the {Vulnerable} state lose Temporary {STR} equal to [StateArg1]% of the wielder's ATK. If the current Realm is {Caro}, after the wielder Unleashes "Exalt", {Embryo Fusion} +[StateArg2].
wheel-0003.lore:
Gloomy clouds shrouded Londinium, blocking the moonlight. The midnight chimes have rung, and the towering chimneys continue to spew hot black smoke and steam. Snowflakes, touched by the dust in the wind, are stained with a dull gray before touching the ground; they flutter down onto the streetlamp, almost instantly melting from the heat of the light and dripping onto the girl passing by.
The girl wears a brown dress, with oversized sleeves carelessly rolled up, revealing her bony arms. She struggles to jog, occasionally pulling up her sleeves, trying hard to catch up with the woman ahead.
The woman glances sideways at the girl and clicks her tongue: "Hurry up, don't think that just because you walk slowly you don't have to go." 
"Okay, okay, Mom," the girl quickly runs up beside the woman, panting, "Sorry, Mom." 
"Don't call me Mom!" The woman grabs the girl's arm and drags her along faster, "You bloodsucking waste, if it weren't for you, I wouldn't have sunk to this wretched place." 
"Sorry..." 
"Shut up, useless thing." The woman's nails dig into the girl's arm, leaving a line of deep marks, "I tell you, from now on the Fang is your home, don't even think about coming out to find me; I don't want to see you even if it kills me, got it?" 
"I... I understand." The girl lowers her head, tears falling beneath her shoes, disappearing into the filthy slush along the Tablet road. In the distance, the factory sounds its closing whistle, a stray cat darts through the alley, the subtle noise is swallowed up by the footsteps of the two. They turn the corner, passing a yard with clothes hanging that exude dampness, and finally stop in front of a building with metal gates.
"You wait here," the woman's voice is very calm, even carrying a hint of detachment, "When dawn breaks, the people inside will come out. They will take you in.
Don't follow me, or you'll see if I kill you." 
The woman turns and leaves, her similarly thin and slightly stooped figure quickly disappearing into the darkness. The girl helplessly clutches her hands, watching the place where the woman left, wiping her tears with her sleeve.
Dim light appears behind her, stretching the shadows of the iron gate and herself. A hand lands on the girl's shoulder, startling her.
"Poor child, why are you here alone?" The newcomer is a young woman dressed in a nun-like robe, "Come, Sister Sarah will take you inside. You won't be cold for long."

wheel-0004.name: By Rose Alone
wheel-0004.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: For every instance of DMG dealt by the wielder, there is a [StateArg3]% chance for the wielder to gain 2% Temporary Crit. DMG. After the wielder releases Exalt, they deal Active DMG 3 times to a random enemy equal to [StateArg1]% of the wielder's ATK. If the current realm is {Caro}, for every {Embryo} {Devoured}, {Embryo Fusion} +[StateArg2].
wheel-0004.lore:
A countryside village girl wearing an extravagant gown, gold and silver jewelry, and a feather fan sneaks into the world of the elite...
Nothing could be more improper than this. A person of lower status trespassing into our ranks is simply scandalous...!
I say, there should be mandatory identification badges indicating their status, so they are not mistaken for one of us.
—Duke Philip Harold

If you live in Londinium, you must be familiar with the so-called "Sorel Effect." 
Still worried that your clothes won't sell? You'd better pray you have some connections to gain the favor of Lady Sorel—yes, even a ridiculously silly pig-tail dress hat, if placed on Lady Sorel's head, will sell like hotcakes.
She is the pop idol of the new era.
—"The Fame Quiz of Lady Sorel," The Lady's Guide

It was a clear summer night.
We sat together on the marble-paved terrace, sipping aged noble botrytis wine, discussing poetry and world peace.
And then something happened @4
Get to the point? Ahem, my apologies...
In short, this woman cheated me out of three thousand Leo Fang, two properties in Soreland, and a sincere heart!
—A heartbroken viscount who wishes to remain anonymous

wheel-0005.name: Chains Unbound
wheel-0005.descriptionTemplate:
The Base DMG of the wielder's cards +[StateArg1]%.
At the start of each turn, there is a [StateArg2]% chance to create a Basic Copy of a random one of the wielder's non-Derived Command Cards; the copy has {Exhaust}.
Whenever an {Embryo} is played targeting the wielder, the Crit. Rate of their next Strike Command +[StateArg3]%.
wheel-0005.lore:
When the hero finally concludes his grand journey, and people close the last page of the picture book and drift off to sleep.

As the long night falls, Helot's story begins.

She travels north along the lengthy coastline, crossing streams and scaling mountains, until she reaches the edge of the frigid land.
Years have passed since the shocking rebellion, where Helot brought peace and prosperity wherever she went. A delicate balance was maintained between slaves and masters, making violent rebellion seem awkward and ridiculous.
She was questioned, mocked, and eventually forgotten.
But she never defended herself.

The warrior Helot came and went like the wind, planting the seeds of freedom in the heart of the Mare Internum. Five years, ten years, a hundred years...
One day, they will break through the soil and flourish.

No one knows where this Spartan killer was laid to rest, though it is said her old friend Epaminondas took care of her remains.
Years later, if you pass by a jasmine-covered grassy knoll, you might see a small tombstone that reads:

Under this grass lies a warrior, or rather,
She once was a warrior.
She is the daughter of old Pericles and the pride of Tilos.
Poet Thracius praised her battle prowess.
And the dark-haired Spartans knew her achievements better.

wheel-0006.name: Drowning in Crimson
wheel-0006.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: The wielder's Base DMG +[StateArg1]%. Before Exalt, Temporary hand size +1, draw 1 Command Card. In {Caro} realm, triggering {Devour} temporarily increases Crit. DMG by [StateArg1]%, once per turn
wheel-0006.lore:
She once questioned countless times in the dead of night: Why must she endure this endless pain?
No one answered.
Her hoarse whispers turned into fragments, never reaching the ears of any god.
Her pain festered, turning into pus and brewing deeper hatred.
One day, she noticed black liquid oozing from the people around her, resembling her own blood.
They quickly descended into madness, just like her.
Perhaps it was their pain; she watched coldly, feeling a faint sense of satisfaction.
But if that was pain, hadn't she long been consumed by the darkness?
She approached the lingering blackness and unexpectedly found freedom.
Yet the freedom brought no relief; she was like a demon exiled to Hell for millennia, her desires corroded by hatred.
Her body bound in shackles, her soul weighed down by lead.
Only the eerie screams could cleanse her heavy soul, allowing her to breathe freely.
Come, taste what I have tasted, and become me.
She found red to be a marvelous color.
She finally understood: Pain has no reason.
Either drink it yourself or invite others to share.
Tonight, she poured the bitter wine, inviting the entire town.

wheel-0007.name: Pale Descendant
wheel-0007.descriptionTemplate:
Shield gained from the wielder +[StateArg3]%.
The wielder's Defense Commands inflict {Poison} equal to [StateArg1]% of their DEF on the enemy with the highest HP.
Whenever {Poison} is inflicted on an enemy, increase {Embryo Fusion} by +[StateArg2], up to 3 times per turn.
wheel-0007.lore:
Wind surges from all directions as you stand at the entrance of the maze with a candle in hand.

You feel a bit lost.

It seems that just a moment ago, you were in Mythag's cafeteria, listening to Charlotte tell tales about the library's underground maze.

And now, you are wandering through one passageway after another in your pajamas, bleary-eyed.

"Charlotte, Charlotte..."

A rustling fills the air as white spiders pour in like a tide, coalescing into a pale girl.

She sits on a swing fashioned from chunks of flesh, swinging back and forth.

"Here you are," she says.

"I'm looking for my friend, Charlotte.
She got lost, and I have to find her..."
You are on the brink of tears.

The girl giggles.
She jumps off the swing and steps toward you.

"Don't you remember? Our deal..."
She places her hand on your belly, then her eyes light up as if she suddenly realizes something.

"Oh, right. You've already become my offspring, so you don't remember anything."

"Offspring...?" 
You look down and feel where your skins touch.

The warm sensation, like spider silk, burrows beneath your skin and threads its way deep into veins.

The girl gives a sly blink and leans close, whispering in your ear:
"Charlotte... is right inside you."

wheel-0008.name: Bloody Feast
wheel-0008.descriptionTemplate:
The Crit. Rate & Crit. DMG of the wielder's Exalt each +[StateArg2]%.
Whenever the wielder's Exalt triggers {Devour}, gain {STR} equal to [StateArg1]% of their CON.
After the wielder Exalts, gain {STR} equal to [StateArg1]% of their CON. If {Devour} was triggered, gain {STR} one more time.
wheel-0008.lore:
For years, I have been thinking about Uvhash.
But it was only among the ruins of these temples that I truly saw him for the first time.
I saw him bound by heavy shackles, forced to lie on the ground, stretching his neck to desperately reach the already spoiled food.
Like a dog.

After Caligula died, as a co-conspirator of the tyrant, he has been locked in the ruins of the temple ever since.
I dragged the chains in my hand, bringing a slave of Torres forward.
He immediately stood upright, his pupils dilating, making a rapid swallowing sound in his throat.

"Wait."
I pulled the chain, bringing the slave to a distance just out of his reach.
"Help me kill someone."
He grinned, revealing a row of sharp beast teeth.
"You are the 19th human to say that to me, and I have to refuse you."
I struggled to keep a straight face, not letting him see my disappointment:
"Why?"
"If you want me to kill a thousand people, I would be more than happy. Killing one? Your bargaining chip is far from enough."
"What if that person is the current emperor?"
I took a deep breath.
"Is this bargaining chip enough?"
Uvhash laughed heartily:
"This is a math problem, my old buddy. One is one, a thousand is a thousand."

"Fire! The Golden Palace is on fire!"
In the distance, roaring flames erupted.
Tyrant, tyrant......
I cried in despair, for Seneca and for the magnificent palace that was about to become ruins.
"Are you that sad?"
Uvhash leaned closer.
He had already enjoyed that Torres slave, satisfying himself as he smacked his lips.
Yes, I promised him that as long as he could kill the tyrant, besides freedom, he could also enjoy a thousand innocent lives.
Could he understand what this fire meant?
Could he understand what it meant to serve an irredeemable tyrant?
Probably not.
He's just a beast.

"Rome is like a ruin," the beast said.
"Or rather, your country, the world......
In my view, it is a meaningless and endless ruin.
You weep for it, kill each other, doing everything futile and pointless......
To be honest, I don't understand."

I looked at his battered body.
This is a golem forged from millions of flesh and blood, shattered and reassembled over the hundreds of years, just like Rome.
"What about you? Are you also a ruin?"
"Perhaps." 
He stretched lazily, golden light from afar danced in his narrow eyes.
"But I don't care."

wheel-0009.name: The Glutton's Tale
wheel-0009.descriptionTemplate:
HP recovered by the wielder +[StateArg2]%.
At the start of each turn, gain Temporary {STR} and Shield equal to [StateArg1]% of the total amount of HP you lost last turn.
wheel-0009.lore:
<Bold:January 24>
I smell terrible.
Today, while riding home with Teresa the housekeeper, we passed through the South Anpus District.
It was not a pleasant experience.
It's a dangerous place. Some people have strange wounds, like triangular holes.
Teresa the housekeeper says it's the punishment from the Arrival for the heretics.

<Bold:February 22>
I've had a fever for a month.
Finally, I ate something today. Fresh steak, tender, juicy...
Yes, juicy.
Red, sweet, and delicious.
All the mirrors at home have been removed, and even Bobby doesn't dare look at me.
Hehe. They don't know that at night, I can see my reflection in the window's shadow.
On my face, neck, chest... from top to bottom, densely packed,
grown with black triangles.

<Bold:March 17>
My cornea feels as if covered with a red veil.
The world turned the color of blood.
Father yelled at me from within the blood mist, the first time he spoke to me so loudly.
He was close, but his voice was distant. I smelled despair, disgust, and blood.
Do not take me away, do not...

wheel-0010.name: Lullaby Devoured
wheel-0010.descriptionTemplate:
At the start of battle, gain {Counter} equal to [StateArg1]% of the wielder's DEF.
Whenever {Death Resistance} triggers, increase {Embryo Fusion} by +[StateArg3] and gain 2 turns of "Cradle": Shield gained from and HP recovered by all Awakeners +[StateArg2]%. Remove 1 stack of "Cradle" at turn end. If the current Realm is {Caro}, {Embryo Fusion} +[StateArg3] after triggering {Death Resistance}.
wheel-0010.lore:
She is called the cursed child born with death, an unblessed daughter. 
The "cradle" belonging to her and her sister has just shattered, and brilliant light spills out from the cracks, taking root on the ground. In an instant, green grass withers, flowers fade, and even distant trees and fish in the lake lose their vitality, becoming soulless husks. 
Her family gasps in horror and hurriedly relocates all other cradles. She struggles to break free from the cradle, like a chick hatching from an egg, but her outstretched hands are repulsed with disgust, and all she sees are faces of contempt. 
Only her sister, who was born that night and matured earlier than her, will forever hold her. She sings lullabies with a voice like a spirit bird. 
"Sleep, sleep, my dear sister; 
Sleep, sleep, sweet dreams will surely come." 

She should not exist, and if she did not, her shimmering sister would not be stained by the shadow of sin. 
My poor sister, she always thinks that you bear the cost of existence you shouldn't have to shoulder. 
My poor sister, she always thinks that as long as the sister can do it, she will surely give her all for you. 
"Sleep, sleep, my dear sister; 
Sleep, sleep, I love you even in dreams."

wheel-0011.name: Kiss of Repose
wheel-0011.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: The wielder's Base DMG +[StateArg1]% During this exploration, every time the wielder kills an enemy, their Crit. Rate +2% and Exalt Base DMG +[StateArg2]%, up to 10 times. If the current Realm is {Caro}, the wielder gains an extra [StateArg4] Aliemus when they directly consume {Embryo} (cooldown 3 turns).
wheel-0011.lore:
Do not fear death. It is merely the body returning the soul to the night, like the tide receding into the quiet dark. True horror is not the end—it is dissolving into nothingness, becoming an unremembered whisper in the void.

She did not die peacefully.

The withered corpse had stiffened, blood and dissolution fluid fused into dark crimson glass. Yet her soul still quivered in the hollow of her throat, a wisp of consciousness clinging like smoke between her bones. A butterfly with shattered wings, beautiful and doomed.

Doresain stepped into the room, leaving the dust undisturbed. He knelt beside her, his gaze tracing her clenched eyelids, her frozen fingers, the restless ghost-light trapped within.

"Am I still alive?"

"Will this darkness swallow me?"

He did not answer. Instead, he pried open her cracked fingers with the care of one unfolding a dying flower. There was no pity in him, no judgment—only the serene patience of a god who deals in endings. And in that moment, she found peace.

A rose between their joined hands—sweet, fragile. He bent low, pressing a kiss to her knuckles. As his lips met her skin, the last shudder of death stretched thin, then melted into the air like a sigh after a storm. No fear. No doubt. Only surrender.

"I will remember you."

He stood, studying her tranquil form. Then—sharp teeth, yielding flesh. Celina's voice curled through his mind:

"I believe in you, brother."

She was fortunate. In Doresain's memory, she would sleep forever.

wheel-0012.name: Paradoxical Realism
wheel-0012.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: The wielder's Aliemus and {STR} Generation +[StateArg1]%. After unleashing Exalt, the Final DMG, Shield, and Healing from other Awakeners' Exalts this turn +[StateArg2]%. If this Exalt triggered {Devour}, the wielder gains [StateArg3] Aliemus.
wheel-0012.lore:
He saw the church spire melting like a candle, dripping down onto the priest's pure white wings.
The saints in the stained glass rotted into skeletons at high speed, then instantaneously regressed into infants.
Sharp and soft geometric shapes were recited from the mouths of the faithful, filling the entire hall.

He saw the train station's space folding repeatedly into non-Euclidean space, the distant platform just within reach, while the nearby ticket gate was impossibly far away.
The clock in the hall rotated counter-clockwise, and the bony crowd walked backwards, pulled by invisible lines, as thick violet smoke drifted from the clouds into the mouth of the train.

He saw the "void" in the forest take shape, with trees, rocks, and vines merely outlining the translucent contour drawn by the "void".
The swan's feathers were constantly changing mosaic colors, and where they swam, the lake water solidified into translucent stained glass.
Beneath the bench where couples cuddled, a vivid orange-yellow shadow was trying to gnaw at its owner's ankle.

He saw the notes of the opera hall beating down on the audience like raindrops, and he saw the graveyard gently rise and fall, like the skin of a breathing giant beast...

He saw a lot, so much. He told what he saw to his mother, teachers, and friends, and painted what he saw into his artwork.
He wanted to bring the world he saw before people, but the number of people around him grew fewer, and one world drifted further from him.

But why is this? He merely recorded what he saw.
Pickman couldn't understand.

Sad Pickman stood before the mirror.
He saw a ghoul grinning at him with sharp, toothy jaws.

"There is nothing more important than witnessing the real. Madness, chaos, greed...
Do you want to see, Pickman?"

The enlightened Pickman smiled at him and stepped into the mirror.

He only wanted to witness the most extreme madness of reality with his own eyes and faithfully depict it on his canvas.

He is neither human nor ghoul.

He is just Pickman, the realist painter.

wheel-0013.name: Vain Regrets
wheel-0013.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: The Fixed {Poison} & {Poison} Trigger effects caused by the wielder +[StateArg1]%. {Caro} Realm's automatic {Embryo Fusion} +[StateArg2]%. When the wielder first triggers {Resonance} each turn, other Awakeners gain [StateArg3] Aliemus.
wheel-0013.lore:
<Italic:"Do you regret it now?">

The ancient deity, reborn from a resplendent glazed goblet, inhaled her scent. It lingered like a ghost upon her shoulder as he pushed open the window to usher in a night breeze laced with a faint fragrance.

"A single furrow of my brow causes beacon fires to flare along the city walls. If I smile, tribute boats from every land sail up the rivers, laden with wonders: rare treasures, exotic beasts, honeyed brews, and delicacies. I am the most precious treasure of the entire dynasty—why should I regret?"

Flushed with wine, she giggled and nestled into plush cushions of silk and fur, drowsy and content.
...
<Italic:"Do you regret it now?">

The ancient deity, bound to a wisp of incense, conversed with him. Faint, illusory smoke drifted down from the pavilion, tracing the outline of a flower bud as its petals unfurled.

"They exhaust themselves in a fever of plots and rivalries, while I reap the rewards without lifting a finger. The higher they climb, the harder they fall—but I? I already have everything. If anyone is due for regret, it certainly isn't me."

With a cheeky grin, he refilled the jug and casually plucked a soft twig to tease the insects chirping in their cage.
...
<Italic:"Do you regret it now?">

The ancient deity, emerging from the well in the form of swirling mist, chuckled softly in her ear. Its tendrils brushed through her hair and shook loose a plump white magnolia from its branch.

"I have love, I have bonds, I have warm congee when I return home at sunset, and I also have fine wine for joyous gatherings. How could I regret? Truly, I do not."

She picked up the <Italic:huadian> to put it on her forehead. The warm, golden candlelight swayed along the hem of her skirt; her gaze clung earnestly to the window, waiting for the one who would return.
...
<Italic:"Do you regret it now?">

The hem of her skirt, once elegant, is now spattered with the foul mire before the Palace Gates. The guards who yesterday offered her fawning gifts now stand deaf to her wailing pleas; two cold halberds cross and fall, sealing away her last path to life. 

The mist heaves a long, lingering sigh. Then, crimson blood blooms upon the snow, only to be utterly buried by the next winter squall.
...
<Italic:"Do you regret it now?"> 

Pearls from the South Seas spill from sandalwood chests, shattering against the green-stone floor. The crisp, clattering music of their fall is more melodious than any music. 

The scrolls he most cherished—those he hung before his desk to admire daily—tumble into the hall. The brushwork of a master's landscape is pierced and shredded by a blade's tip, much like the sovereign lands he ignored as they were poisoned by the fires of war. 

The mist curls in mockery. Then, a fire ignites, and the carved beams and painted rafters are reduced to the dying embers of a fallen dynasty.

<Italic:"Do you regret it now?"> 

The man whose name she once tasted upon her tongue never returns. Along a distant river lie withered bones; upon a silken pillow a beauty waits in vain. 

Did he become a trusted minister to the Emperor, or a consort to the Princess? Did he linger amidst the "flowers" of the pleasure quarters, never to look back? Or was he devoured by famished refugees, serving as mere grain for a year of disaster? 

The mist murmurs in ambiguous tones, yet it never grants her the answer she so desperately seeks.

The most precious treasure in the dynasty's heyday becomes the most cherished burial offering when the dynasty falls.
The most carefree favorite of a flourishing family becomes the most ideal scapegoat when the family declines.

And as for the last story—it is one that is all too common in this world.

wheel-0014.name: Rewinding Time
wheel-0014.descriptionTemplate: Whenever one of the wielder's cards is played, there is a [StateArg1]% chance to return it to your hand from the Discard Pile. This effect triggers only once each turn.
wheel-0014.lore:
The great sword was long and heavy, capable of slicing a person in half with enough force.

The dagger was light and nimble, easy to hide, perfect for a surprise attack.

The curved sword was beautifully arched, the blade sharp, easily embedding itself into flesh and opening a deep gash.

The firearm was quick and domineering, the crossbow silent and swift, various metal parts glinting coldly under the light. Ten-year-old Ramona stood in front of the wall full of weapons, her face showing an unexpected maturity and seriousness for her age.

"Which one do you want?" The voice of her caretaker - no, the father who raised her - came from behind, "Once you choose, I will help you find a teacher."

Which one? The weight of the great sword was both an advantage and a disadvantage; the dagger couldn't handle longer weapons; the curved sword, although long, always inflicted a lot of unnecessary damage; firearms were too loud, and crossbows could easily run out of ammo.

After observing the training ground of Mythag for so many days, she knew she could spot the weaknesses of both combatants at a glance and nearly instinctively plan the attack routes. Those things that required abundant experience to know could be deduced in just a second for her. No, what she needed was something more precise, a weapon that could inflict the greatest damage with the least force.

The girl pondered for a moment, moved a stool from the side, stood on it, and reached for the rapier hanging high above.

Long, elegant, agile, and lightweight, it could accurately inflict harm - yes, that's it, it met all her requirements

wheel-0015.name: Deathless Ascent
wheel-0015.descriptionTemplate:
HP recovered by the wielder's Command Cards +[StateArg2]%.
After the wielder Exalts, grant all other Awakeners [StateArg1] Aliemus. If Awakeners were {Vulnerable} before the Exalt was released, Aliemus granted this way is doubled.
wheel-0015.lore:
They are born from humanity's imagination of the unknown, yet they do not possess the hysteria and clawing behavior often imagined.

They are rational; they are calm.

They come for the future, they come for existence.

They are the most unusual, the most incredible miracles.

At first, their voice was like the chirping of insects. But after fully comprehending their thoughts, that voice fell silent.

Yet afterward, the masters of the voice attempted to influence every decision she made.

In her first year as an <Awakener>, she learned to coexist with the voices in her head.

She plunged into consciousness, becoming part of the gestalt, seizing the information and knowledge she needed, and finally disengaging safely to return to reality.

She did the same when the "Black Plague" came for this body.

She aimed to separate the body from knowledge, memory, and soul, making the brain an immortal entity.

After the surgery, she nearly entered a vivid and wonderful sleep—but within mere seconds, her thoughts began to operate once more.

She knew clearly that she was approaching that absolute destruction surpassing form, power, and the chaos of symmetry.

wheel-0016.name: Manikin of Oblivion
wheel-0016.descriptionTemplate:
{Poison} inflicted, Aliemus generated, and HP recovered by the wielder each +[StateArg1]%.
After the wielder Exalts, they gain Aliemus equal to [StateArg2]% of the Aliemus consumed by that Exalt. {(Aliemus generation bonuses, such as this Wheel, can affect this amount.)}
wheel-0016.lore:
People always like to define boundaries and symbols based on their own standards.
How narrow-minded, ignorant, and pathetic it is to confine the world within human flesh.
She abandoned her physical form early on, yet still could not reach the true boundaries.
The river of knowledge is chaotic and endless, and the black void has once again caught up with her.
Silently, she confronted it. If tangible aspects cannot reveal the totality of the world, could the void behind it hold the true answer?
She staked her life in the most insane gamble.
She finally saw the end.
The gate of truth opened wide, and the face of the world was instantly overturned.
So it is, so it is.
She desperately wanted everyone to see the truth within the void and be freed from their futile struggles.
Once, she stood atop the waves, leading the world forward.
Now, she still wields the shepherd's whip, joyfully guiding it into the Abyss.
People, terrified, expelled her, calling her a madwoman.
How strange, she thought.
But in my eyes, you are no different.

wheel-0017.name: Heart of a Knight
wheel-0017.descriptionTemplate:
Shield gained from the wielder's Exalt +[StateArg2]%.
After the wielder Exalts, gain Temporary {STR} equal to [StateArg1]% of the wielder's DEF. If Awakeners were {Fragile} before the Exalt was released, {STR} gained this way is doubled.
wheel-0017.lore:
When the Duke appointed him a knight, the boy had witnessed only seven cycles of the four seasons.

Though the Duke's gesture was born of a whimsical tease, the court took the oath to heart. The veteran knights honed his skills in horseback archery, the attendants drilled him in the nuances of etiquette, and the Princess—above all others—placed her absolute trust in her young protector.

While strolling across the wild highlands of Aequidan and into the deep gorge, the young knight led the Princess's horse by the reigns. She lay weakly across the saddle, a hidden darkness slowly devouring her body from within.

"Princess, we can't go any further," he whispered.

To cross the gorge and the river beyond was to abandon the very soil that nurtured them. Outside their borders, war raged; the enemy watched their fertile lands with a hungry, predatory gaze. Only the great chasm had preserved their peace, allowing this small fragment of the world to maintain the dignity of a "nation."

From the safety of the cliffs, they had watched the downfall of those across the water. The pleas of the innocent drowned in the river, destined only to become the tragic meter of the victors' poetry.

But no matter how great the nation, none can truly escape the currents of history.

The enemy eventually came. They severed the knight's tender hands, ensuring he could never again raise spear or shield. They crushed his fragile knees, forcing him to watch in helpless agony as the cavalry smashed through the palace doors. Even then, the knight struggled, biting at the legs of the enemy's warhorses, a desperate and futile effort to halt their atrocities.

Then, for the first time, he saw the "black plague" the Princess had warned of.

A flood of dark liquid burst from the palace doors. It surged over the cavalry's greaves, yet the invaders did not falter. The "black plague" rose, filling the knight's mouth and nose and choking his lungs, yet he fought the urge to succumb.

Then, a familiar voice—gentle and known—echoed in the chambers of his mind. "You've done enough... my most loyal knight."

In the end, only a vast, silent blankness remained.

wheel-0018.name: Celestial Beast
wheel-0018.descriptionTemplate:
The Base DMG of the wielder's Exalt and Strike Commands each +[StateArg1]%.
Whenever one of their Strike Commands is played, the wielder's Crit. Rate +5% this turn. This effect stacks up to 3 times, and when stacks reach 3, the wielder's Crit. DMG +15% this turn.
wheel-0018.lore:
Time flowed, and Lotan could no longer clearly recall her memories of the planet's birth.

She only remembered that she was a chosen being.


This was a story that took place before all concepts had arisen.

"Life" had not yet taken root, and the "Gods" were still far beyond the heavens.

Within this chaotic nebula, Chaos collided with Chaos, and matter gradually took form amidst the impacts and turbulence.

Stars gathered dust, rocks rose into mountainous shapes, and atmospheres condensed into vast bodies of water.

Then, this place was declared a "planet."


The soul of Chaos, trapped within the cage of time, crashed against the newborn soil.

Unable to control its savage nature, driven by the urge to destroy, It toppled countless mountains and filled countless rivers and lakes.

Then—The "Gods" crossed the distant stars and descended upon the barren world.


The "Gods" brought the seeds of civilization, and also imprisoned the soul of Chaos within a "structured" body.

"Make a pact with me..."

Facing the roaring Spirit of Chaos, the white-robed "God" whispered.

"A great civilization will be born here."

"You will meet wise but inactive philosophers, and clash with uncertain blades... In the future, you will no longer be ruled by destruction and devastation."

"Until that day comes... Until we are reunited, protect this place for me."

wheel-0019.name: Hour of Fortune
wheel-0019.descriptionTemplate:
At the start of battle, the Crit. Rate of the wielder's Command Cards +[StateArg1]% for each Relic acquired.
After the wielder Exalts, roll a 4-sided die; they gain +X% Temp. Crit. DMG & {X} Aliemus, where X is [StateArg2] times the result. If the result is 4 or higher, also grant other Awakeners half of this effect.
wheel-0019.lore:
Follow the Taym downstream, turn right onto Beckman Street, walk about a hundred feet, and you'll find the "Ryker Detective Agency" tucked in an unassuming corner.

Despite being Londinium's most successful detective agency, it has no bookshelves crammed with professional tomes, no walls plastered with clippings, and certainly no elegant afternoon tea.

Most likely, you'll only find an "Back Soon" sign on the door and a tired old dog curled up beside it.

If you're lucky, on a clear Sunday morning, you might catch the famous detective returning from a night of drinking.
He is fresh from a gambling table, his pockets nearly empty, and that's when you can throw him any requests: finding a lost cat, rescuing a mother who joined a cult, gathering evidence of an affair...
If you've got the fee, he'll take the case.

"I enjoy those moments right before death, and that's why I was born to be a gambler."
"And a detective? I must say, I don't see how finding a cat relates to dying."
"No, you don't get it... The search for truth is captivating in itself—big case or small. It's like..." 
"Like heading to a date with death."

wheel-0020.name: Aberrant Devour
wheel-0020.descriptionTemplate:
The Base DMG of the wielder's Exalt +[StateArg1]%.
At the start of investigation, the wielder's Scholar alter begins to front. After the wielder Exalts, they switch between Scholar and Jester.
{Scholar} — The wielder gains [StateArg2] Aliemus at the end of each turn.
{Jester} — The wielder's Crit. Rate & Crit. DMG +[StateArg3]% at the end of each turn.
wheel-0020.lore:
A.F.@1/06/05
Albinor brought back some rare Silvers from the D-Rift. Truly impressive.
I made some improvements to the purification method.
This time, I introduced a sufficient amount of carbon dioxide and chlorine gas, followed by low-temperature evaporation and crystallization...
These mechanical steps aren't worth recording in detail.

A.F.@1/06/06
I dissected the internal organs and limbs of specimen 024.
This material was brought back by Albinor from the clinic and has generated 13 dissolution holes, with a maximum diameter of 8mm.
Cutting the parasitic organisms of the OS-106 D-Rift monster.
According to the comparison of various test data, it shows the highest resonance compatibility with 024.
The only issue is... those tentacles are a bit too "lively."
I think I won't want to eat octopus for a long time.
I hope the final result doesn't disappoint me.

@1 Year 6 Month 6 Day
The grafting experiment succeeded, but the vital signs of the specimen disappeared.
This is a common occurrence; we just need to ensure that the grafting site hasn't spread infection.
Sent for a week of observation—what follows won't be my responsibility anymore.
Today, I read two articles criticizing living Awakener experiments in the "Internal Journal."
Needless to say, the signatures reveal they are my old friends from Mythag.
It's laughable—if these old fogeys would just move their hefty butts and visit the lab, they'd know my conclusions are unshakable.
The experiment with 024 will serve as an excellent counterattack.

A.F.@1/06/22
Two weeks have passed, and the vital signs of 024 remain unstable—or rather, the personality within her is dividing and multiplying rapidly like cellular mitosis.
The experiment has failed in an astonishing manner.
Send 024 to the wax museum; I don't need her following me daily, reminding me of my foolishness and rashness.

wheel-0021.name: Memory Spiral
wheel-0021.descriptionTemplate:
Shield gained from the wielder +[StateArg1]%.
At turn start, {Retain} [StateArg2]% of unbroken Shield, up to [StateArg3]% of Max HP.
wheel-0021.lore:
Pain, intense pain filled her every sense.

The metal diving suit was insignificant under the water pressure, its shell buckling inward, screws bursting. Something pressed against her abdomen, something pierced her arm. Cold seawater squeezed in through the damaged suit, and only the thickened metal helmet barely held its form, preserving a meager amount of oxygen for her.

Through the lone peephole of the helmet, she watched as the water outside turned from azure to ink blue, and from ink blue to pitch black. The vapor from her breath condensed on the glass, and the roar of the whirlpool gradually faded, becoming mere phantasms in her ears. She gradually lost sensation in her limbs, her breathing growing heavier.

She guessed she must have fainted, from cold, blood loss, and lack of oxygen; any would be enough to cloud her consciousness. Yet she was unsure because, no matter how much she blinked, all she saw was the same darkness.

This was a place where light could not reach, an uncharted domain in Aequor.

A dull excitement finally stirred her blood—an unknown domain, the final adventure. Wouldn't she have fulfilled her dream? Even though she couldn't see anything now.

A strange sensation came from her ankle. Instinctively looking down, she discovered that, in the darkness, something seemed to climb up her leg. Was it a peculiar deep-sea creature, colder than the sea itself? How... in such darkness, she seemed able to see its outline?

Black, writhing, like sludge...
What is this? Where is she? Who... is she?

wheel-0022.name: Gift of Decay
wheel-0022.descriptionTemplate:
{Poison} inflicted by the wielder +[StateArg1]%.
As part of the wielder's Exalt, first they inflict {Poison} on all enemies equal to [StateArg2]% of their ATK; then resolve the Exalt's effects.
Whenever you unleash a Posse, the wielder gains [StateArg3] Aliemus.
wheel-0022.lore:
"People are born with the tide and die with the ebb."
This is a legend that has been passed down in Nymphaea's hometown.

The villagers of Sevada do not fear death; to them, the sprouting and wilting of life are like the rise and fall of the tide, both part of nature.
They love the blue sky, the ocean, the flocks of seagulls, and the calla lilies blooming in the morning sun.
They despise steam, iron tracks, and all things too heavily marked by human intervention—interference with life is included in that disdain.
To cut one's life short is unacceptable in the eyes of the Sevadians.

Nymphaea is not a typical Sevadian.
For instance, she is always drawn to the atmosphere of shipwrecks.
A dead shipwreck is home to countless lives: fish, water plants, various shellfish...
Here, life and death intertwine in a mysterious and sacred manner.
With calmness, Nymphaea approaches the wreck, as if stepping into an immensely solemn cathedral.

During the years when her father took her on trips to relax, Nymphaea saw a five-act play.
The content of that play has long faded from her memory; it seemed to tell the life of an ancient emperor.
He lived a chaotic life filled with military campaigns north and south, only to die from a shameful conspiracy.
She remembers one line:
<Italic:Cowards die many times before their deaths; the valiant never taste of death but once.>

Years later, when Nymphaea steps into the cold seawater, pained by her longing for life, that line floats through her mind:
"The valiant never taste of death but once..."
So she wipes her tears, says goodbye to her parents one last time, and continues towards the shipwreck in her heart.
She will become her own warrior.

wheel-0023.name: Drifting in Desire
wheel-0023.descriptionTemplate:
{Counter} gained from the wielder +[StateArg3]%.
Whenever an enemy attacks you, gain Temporary {STR} equal to [StateArg1]% of the wielder's ATK for each hit.
After the wielder Exalts, gain {Counter} equal to [StateArg2]% of the wielder's ATK.
wheel-0023.lore:
Tap, tap, tap.
Footsteps approach. A gentleman in a top hat walks through the ruins, standing before a dying guard.
Gentleman: What are you doing here?

(The gentleman suffers from severe stuttering, while the guard likes to beat around the bush. For your reading convenience, only the key points of the conversation are recorded.)

Guard: I'm guarding the prison.
The gentleman looked around the deserted field and the slowly collapsing ruins tainted with dissolution.
Gentleman: But there is no prison here, nor prisoners. Your kingdom no longer exists.
Guard: I'm guarding the prison.
Gentleman: Why?
The guard lifted her head, a childlike face filled with confusion and determination.
Guard: I don't know... I want to go home.
Guard: I'm guarding the prison for that lord. If the lord is pleased, I can go home.
She doesn't even know who that lord is or why she should guard it.
The gentleman doesn't ask further, as if it were the most natural thing.
Gentleman: What is your hometown like?
Guard: It's only winter and night there, with endless forests and seas.
Gentleman: Doesn't sound livable.
Guard: I hate daylight. Do you see? I'm made of wax. If I touch the sun, I will disappear.
Guard: So I hide here, unable to go home by myself.
The gentleman nods thoughtfully. Suddenly, he becomes excited.
Gentleman: What a coincidence! You're made of wax, and I happen to be a wax sculptor.
Gentleman: Let's make a pact—I can let you walk in daylight and help you find your way home. In return, you will be my assistant.
Guard: Assistant, what's that?
Gentleman: You can guard my wax museum for me. It's different from this prison; it's art, and art never collapses.
Guard: Is art that powerful?
The gentleman laughs happily.
Gentleman: Yes, it is that powerful.

At the end of the story, the guard followed the gentleman muddleheadedly, just as she had come to this prison muddleheadedly.
She has always been someone who goes with the flow.

wheel-0024.name: Grace Through Pain
wheel-0024.descriptionTemplate:
{Poison} inflicted by and Shield gained from the wielder each +[StateArg3]%, and the Crit. Rate & Crit. DMG of the wielder's Strike Commands +[StateArg1]%.
The first time one of their Strike Commands is played each turn, gain Shield equal to [StateArg2]% of Max HP.
wheel-0024.lore:
"What is memory?" 
"It is what you have forgotten." 

Lily has a forgetful mother. 
She forgets to call Lily for meals, forgets to give Lily her medicine, and even forgets Lily's name. 
"What should I do?" 
Lily sat on the hospital bed, pretending to be deep in thought, and sighed. 
Such a mother is truly worrying. 

Later, Lily wrote her name on a wooden board and hung it around her neck every day. 
That should help her remember, right? 
At first, her mother could still laugh and say she was overreacting, that it was just a complication from long-term fatigue. 

Gradually, she began to show confusion and impatience: 
"Lily... what is Lily?" 
Amber eyes gazed at Lily, carrying a hint of veiled caution. 
It turned out that when the meaning of words is forgotten, the harmony temporarily maintained through language will ultimately disappear. 

Lily hugged her mother. 
The heartbeats of mother and daughter were merely inches apart, yet she felt extremely lonely. 

After that day, Lily put away the wooden board, no longer indulged in her mother's affection, and stopped reminding her that she had gone hungry for many days. 
She was a sick patient on the brink of death, numbly waiting for the verdict of death. 
She was a distant spectator, watching herself being injected, anesthetized, and manipulated into any form her mother wished her to be. 
The happy words exchanged between mother and daughter, the soft embraces... 
Those joyful memories flashing like dreamy bubbles were carefully locked in Lily's heart. 
Only on some weary nights did she reluctantly take them out and gently savor them.

wheel-0025.name: Will Unyielding
wheel-0025.descriptionTemplate:
The wielder's Base DMG and Shield gained from them each +[StateArg1]%.
At the start of investigation, add 1 of the wielder's Basic Strike and 1 of their Basic Defense to the deck.
Whenever one of their Strike Commands is played, Shield gained from their next Defense Command +15%;
Whenever one of their Defense Commands is played, the Crit. Rate & Crit. DMG of their next Strike Command +15%.
These effects can stack up to 3 times, and persist between battles.
wheel-0025.lore:
"Retreat, retreat!"
"Retreat to @3! Move quickly!"
"Help, help me! Don't abandon me!"
"Leave him behind, retreat now—Alva!"
The soldier wielding the gunblade surged forward, explosions erupting around her, blending with the clamor of battle, screams, and the blaring war horn. Blood drenched the earth, its stench overpowering even the smell of gunpowder. Lifeless soldiers lay scattered, their uniforms dyed a vivid red regardless of their original colors.
Alva clenched her teeth, pushing forward against the relentless onslaught of enemies. She spotted him—a young man crying for help, pinned to the ground, desperately fending off a towering enemy's dagger.
A deafening gunshot and searing pain struck simultaneously; the nearby enemy collapsed slowly. Alva spun around and slashed, enduring the agony in her leg as she lunged forward, driving her blade into her opponent's chest and pulling the trigger.
Blood sprayed everywhere; she hastily wiped her face, only to realize the young man was gone.
She glanced down—a knife protruded from her left knee, blood gushing out.
Tearing a strip of cloth from her cloak to staunch the bleeding, she used her gunblade as a makeshift crutch and limped after her unit.

wheel-0026.name: Silent Banquet
wheel-0026.descriptionTemplate:
{Poison} inflicted by the wielder +[StateArg3]%.
After the wielder Exalts, recover HP equal to [StateArg1]% of their CON, plus another [StateArg2]% of their CON for each unspent Arithmetica.
wheel-0026.lore:
The Erivinian people reside in the gray stone city, adjacent to a green lake. 

The lake has no name; it has existed here since the Erivinians have memory. 
It is said that an evil hag is sealed deep within the waters of the lake. 
She is as ancient as the lake, a terrifying existence crudely shaped at the dawn of creation. 
Naive and timid, the Erivinians fear the hag, attempting to appease her to ensure she remains quietly at the bottom of the lake. 

"What should we do?" 
"Yes, what should we do?" 
"Then let's dance! Dancing is always good, after all." 

Thus, on every full moon, the Erivinians gather by the lake to dance gracefully before a paper effigy. 
In those moments, with the moon shining bright and the stars sparse, all is peaceful. 

Hundreds of years later, the Erivinians forget their history, forget their fables, and forget their effigies. 
During the millennium celebration of the stone city, they indulge in raucous laughter and drink fine wine. 
The noisy sounds are cast into the lake, like stones hitting water, spreading in endless circles from the center of the lake, ultimately reaching the depths. 
Thus, on a full moon, the Erivinians hear sounds beyond the range of their hearing and see the moon and flames beyond the range of their vision. 
They also become a part of the silence. 

This infinite silence within endless space fills the Erivinians with fear. 
They are forced to sit still like mystics, watching a new tribe laugh and drink in exuberance before them, coming and going in cycles. 
So the universe will continue to expand forever, the galaxies will drift further apart, and all things will perish. 
That's just how it is. 
Whether the Erivinians feel lonely about this is of no importance.

wheel-0027.name: Wheel Unseen
wheel-0027.descriptionTemplate:
Hand Limit +2. {(Does not stack with other Wheels.)}
Keyflare Cap +100%.
The wielder's Keyflare Regen +[StateArg1]%.
After one of the wielder's cards is played, the Crit. Rate & Crit. DMG of, Aliemus generated by, and Shield & {STR} gained from the wielder's cards each +[StateArg2]% this turn.
(This effect stacks up to 5 times.)
wheel-0027.lore:
When someone knows all of the past and future, they are met not with joy but with endless sorrow and despair.
Her eyes see the threads behind everything—one end linked to the past, the other to the future.
These threads are called fate.
How does she view those around her?
People are kind and loving, yet arrogant and doubtful.
But their fates, as well as humanity's destiny, are sealed from the moment they meet.
She must love them; otherwise, why would she repeatedly offer help and give them fire?
She must hold expectations for them; otherwise, why would she walk into the shadow of reincarnation amidst destruction?
Because of love, she feels sorrow.
Because of expectation, she often feels disappointment.
She is deeply trapped in a black swamp, tirelessly sorting through countless black threads.
The wheel of fate turns again and again.
She sees greedy flames sweep across the sky.
She sees blind stubbornness leading to self-destruction.
She sees insane delusions accelerating destruction.
Chaos will always come.
Hope and despair rotate in vain, endlessly.
Until she sees a fleeting starlight in the darkness.
That light, though faint, shines powerfully in her long night.
In that moment of seeing it, she sheds a tear.

wheel-0028.name: Amber-Tinted Death
wheel-0028.descriptionTemplate:
The wielder's Base DMG +[StateArg1]%, and {STR} gained from them +[StateArg2]%.
Whenever one of the wielder's Command Cards is played, gain Temporary {STR} equal to [StateArg3]% of their ATK.
(This effect stacks up to 5 times.)
wheel-0028.lore:
Most of the time, death always arrives silently, before a human's consciousness has a chance to react. The reaper would have already crept close, claiming that tender, fragile life.

However, it is said that in a remote desert unknown to most, there exists an amber-tinted death god. She brings Amber-Tinted Death with her amber fire and amber gun, after issuing a formal proclamation.

The man stood on the sandy sea beneath the moonlit night, his hand gripping the firearm trembling ceaselessly. He stared intently at Kathigu-Ra's amber silhouette without blinking, not daring to relax for even a moment.

Cold sweat dripped from his forehead, falling heavily into the hearts of everyone behind him. He had brought many people along, but at this moment, very few of them were actually useful.

He couldn't understand—this was supposed to be a simple mission to loot an abandoned warehouse, so why had he encountered the legendary death god?

"You mean the death god? That's just an idle rumor from before," Kathigu-Ra said indifferently.

"I used to coat them in amber; perhaps you could still find some leftover paint clinging to the surface?"

She generously displayed the twin guns in her hands to the person before her.

"The amber-colored flame at the muzzle is just a special substance I sprinkled on."

Her finger plunged into the barrel, rotating once around before pulling out some unknown powder.

"The so-called death god is merely an image I once crafted for myself."

"It was powerful, mysterious, and omnipotent."

"It helped me intimidate those incompetent petty villains."

She spoke arrogantly, completely unconcerned about the increasingly fierce gaze from the man before her.

She spoke as if no one else was present, entirely unaware that danger was already approaching.

When the bullets finally rained down upon her, shock and bewilderment flickered in her eyes—

merely to conceal that subtle hint of excitement and madness buried deep within her subconscious.

...

The gunfire ceased, and everything unfolded exactly as she had anticipated.

She stood at the center of the battlefield, everything around her scorched and marked by fire.

Kathigu-Ra performed only two actions—raising the gun, and firing.

When she lifted the spear, death danced behind all things, equally casting its omen upon all.

When she pulled the trigger, flames engulfed everything, offering the world a sea of amber blossoms.

"They have accompanied me through many years, bearing silent witness to endless bloodshed and death."

"They were once forced to wear the disguise of lies, used to hide my own weakness and helplessness."

"Now, they are no longer just rumors."

wheel-0029.name: Doomsday Rampage
wheel-0029.descriptionTemplate:
The Base DMG of the wielder's Exalt and {Pursuit} each +[StateArg1]%.
After any Awakener's Strike Command is played, DMG dealt by the wielder's Strike Commands this turn increases by [StateArg2]% of the wielder's ATK.
(This effect stacks up to 8 times.)
wheel-0029.lore:
"What kind of fun happens when a world is heading towards its end?"

Mouchette didn't know, but she was looking forward to it.

This anticipation was different from before; this time it wasn't just about having a bit of fun with some Ancient Monsters, it was research and investigation measured on the scale of an entire world. And at the core of this research and investigation was one person—
 the Keeper of Mythag.

"I will save this world..."

"This world will definitely not meet a fate of collapse..."

That person, who held ideals and bore the fate of a world, made such a promise. In response, Mouchette knew she should show a moved expression; it was the social etiquette established in human society.

But she didn't want to pretend anymore, or rather, after witnessing the Keeper confront @4, sacrificing themselves to save a world, she felt there was no need to pretend in front of someone who had experienced cosmic horror.

"Pretending" was the game rule of human society—decent yet boring—while the universe... the universe was real and straightforward—
 either survive or perish.

Thus, her response to the Keeper was also genuine and straightforward.

"I don't care if this world will be saved or destroyed... Compared to that, I am more curious about you."

I am curious whether you can fulfill your ideals or if you will crumble on the path of pursuing them.

I am also curious whether this world will respond to your wishes or inevitably head towards destruction.

No matter which outcome, it will be a grand and rare form of fun.

I will always look forward to that day...

with my eyes and my axe, waiting.

wheel-0030.name: Fouetté
wheel-0030.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: The wielder's Active DMG +an amount equal to the wielder's ATK ×[StateArg3]%. After playing or discarding 1 of the wielder's Command Cards, deal DMG equal to the wielder's ATK ×[StateArg1]% as {Poison} to a random enemy once. After the {Ultra Round} ends, there is a [StateArg2]% chance to trigger 100% {Poison} on all enemies.
wheel-0030.lore:
On a grand stage, a young girl is dancing.
Her hair is tightly coiled into a bun, adorned with white feathers and a delicate crown. Her pure white dress flares out with her movements, twirling gracefully to the mournful sound of violins.
With arms extended and toes pointed, the white swan lowers its neck, sharing its sorrow and grief with the prince.
Young Liz watches with her mouth slightly open, captivated by the graceful figure on stage. That dancer is her cousin, who came to live with them before Liz was born and trained rigorously under their grandmother. Usually plain and quiet, her cousin has never appeared so vibrant, like a swan ready to take flight. She, along with everyone else in the theater, is entranced by her cousin's Shadow.
Liz's heart beats faster and faster—how enviable! She too wants to be that swan, to leave her mark on the eternal stage. She knows she can do it. Didn't her grandmother praise her long, straight legs when she thought Liz wasn't listening? Didn't her cousin, the former prima ballerina, say she had great potential? Yes, once she starts, once she puts on those pointe shoes, people will come from distant cities to watch her perform. She will be the greatest dancer of this century, a shining star in the world of ballet!
At that time, she didn't know the only thing awaiting her was scorching flames.

wheel-0031.name: Treasured Rarity
wheel-0031.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: Gain [StateArg1] Black Sigils each time an enemy dies. After the wielder kills an enemy, the wielder's Crit. DMG in this level permanently +[StateArg2]%, stacking up to 5 stacks. Each time the wielder triggers the {Leap} effect, increase the Final DMG dealt by the wielder this turn by [StateArg3]%, stacking up to 3 stacks.
wheel-0031.lore:
The first time you saw a living golem was at the scholar lady's house.

"Even now, she still amazes me. To me, she is the universe itself."

The golem gives you a mechanical smile, muttering incomprehensibly.

You gaze into her gray eyes, feeling like you are in a surging gray ocean, the shimmering waves moving from one horizon to another.

Before leaving, the scholar lady hands you a worn booklet with notes on making golems.

That night, you spread it out to study the scribbled formulas and spells, but your mind is filled with the golem's elusive smile.

You never forget her.

Days later, you find an excuse to visit again.

You step over piles of books, tools, and skeletons, pull aside heavy curtains and entwined cobwebs, finally squatting before the golem.

The scholar lady is huddled in a corner, looking haggard and drained of light in just a few days.

"You came... just like them."

The scholar lady coughs weakly.

"Yes... I came."

You pick up the golem's hand and kiss it repeatedly.

Its cool fingertips brush against your neck, causing a slight pain. You close your eyes.

When you wake up, you are bathed in warm white sunlight, your body fluctuating with gentle waves.

You feel unprecedented lightness, as if your soul has left your body.

You turn your head and see gray-black heads.

They wail, twist, like dark wildflowers scattered across the gray primeval ocean.

"No—"

Your throat chokes out black mucus, and you can no longer move.

It turns out you have become one of those heads.

"In June of Year 2, renowned alchemist Romalin Ludwig was found mysteriously dead in his study, his head missing. The mysterious alchemy manual also disappeared..."

(This content is recorded in Volume 2 of "Alchemy Anecdotes" from Mythag Library)

wheel-0032.name: Song of the Bound
wheel-0032.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: At the start of exploration, add one "Insight" and the wielder's "Strike" to the deck. the wielder's "Strike" Base DMG +[StateArg1]%, and after playing, there is a [StateArg2]% chance to draw 1 card. This effect can trigger a maximum of once per turn. If the current Realm is {Ultra} and during the {Ultra Round}, the "Strike" will place 1 "Insight" into hand, triggering a maximum of once during that turn.
wheel-0032.lore:
Humans are creatures who love to sing, and so are the people of Wild Star.

Melodies rise freely from their throats, without any intention or purpose.

Scattered by the wind into stray notes, they fade away with passing emotions.

It is said that every wandering tribe in the desert has its own songs.

But the Desolate-born lost theirs.

Along with their song, they also lost their name.

No one knows what the planet was originally called.

It is a cursed world, scorched by drought, storms, famine...

Disasters came one after another, until the planet was utterly broken.

The rich and powerful, dressed in finery, boarded arks and left.
The planet became desolate, home only to the unblessed, the lowly.

All of them were abandoned.

Since then, the Desolate-born decided they needed no laughter, no poetry, and no songs.

They will remember the pain of abandonment, forever crawling through the white desert, until the day the planet ends.

wheel-0033.name: Queen's Edict
wheel-0033.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: The wielder's {Counter} Generation +[StateArg4]%. After playing a card, gain {Counter} equal to [StateArg1]% of the wielder's DEF, up to 2 times per turn. If the card played enters {Ultra Space}, the wielder also gains [StateArg2] Aliemus. After releasing Exalt, all enemies lose [StateArg3]% Temporary {STR} equal to the wielder's DEF.
wheel-0033.lore:
The exact origins of Wanda are widely debated among the locals of Cambrian.

Some say they have seen Wanda sitting on a giant stone sinking into the lakeside.
A man say she is a pharaoh from Remoushe; he once saw with the same spine needle on a mummy.
Still, some say she is a duchess from the Franco Highlands, who inherited a vast fortune after her four husbands all died in succession.
..

Rumors blew away like the wind, and the villagers continued to farm, weave, and grind.
In those hard years, heavy taxes hung overhead like a razor, cutting off all the budding branches and illusions.
Only... whenever passing by the lake area where the queen resides, the music from the thicket easily captivated everyone's hearts.
That was a melody that shouldn't be here.
It was too pleasant, too lazy, making it seem evil, like a devil's whisper.

"Come, cast all this aside."
"The merciful queen sits at the lake's center, waiting for the service of her blind servants..."

So, on a windless night, the weary villagers put down their tools and decided to satisfy their curious desire.
He circumvented the forest, crossed the small bridge, the path blooming with flowers, not a glance spared.
He saw the green lake.
It connected to the low-hanging starry sky, with an ancient castle rising at the edge.

The thorn-crowned queen sat in the center of the lake, patiently awaiting the pilgrims.
She extended her hand, enjoying the kisses and worship of mere mortals.
The dark clouds dispersed, the bright moon rose.
The queen drove the spine needle into his back.
The paralyzing liquid spread through his veins, and he knelt on the ground, dismembered and gouged of his eyes.

At this point, the heavy gravity from the earth could no longer attract him, all his poetry and dreams were solely tethered to that queen.
He finally welcomed his destiny.

wheel-0034.name: Incalculable Factor
wheel-0034.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: The Aliemus generated by the wielder +[StateArg1]%. The wielder gains [StateArg2] Keyflare after releasing Exalt.
wheel-0034.lore:
The towering spire collapsed with a thunderous roar, books written by scholars from different races across different times scattered all around. In an instant, black triangles of all sizes covered each book and every brick, quickly merging into one and spreading outwards.
Black slime flowed like lava along the orderly streets as people screamed and cried, attempting to escape this terrifying disaster. Many tried to use transportation to flee but crashed into buildings or other vehicles that had encountered accidents in the chaos; more people were caught off guard, running away from the slime only to be knocked over by others equally terrified and ultimately assimilated by the slime. Children cried loudly on the streets, while several young people hid in the remnants of buildings, deluding themselves that they could evade death this way.
"There was no warning, why, why!"
"Why didn't anyone warn us!"
"@1, it's dissolution! Help!"
"Quick, initiate consciousness transfer! It's the only way—ah!"
"@2, hurry, use the device!" said a voice, "We'll buy you time!"
"No! I—"
"We've already been @1, but you, you still have hope." answered a gentle voice, "Survive, @2, survive. Then—"

Winkle awoke abruptly, her eyes snapping open to find herself surrounded by stacks of books and several coffee-stained mugs. On the nearby blackboard were notes and memos of varying sizes, along with an equation only partially completed. In the disheveled room, lights were placed in corners and at the center, as well as beside the large device she was at, illuminating the entire office brilliantly.
She remained silent for a moment, curling her body more into the positioning device, continuing the interrupted calculations.

wheel-0035.name: The Last Verse
wheel-0035.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: The wielder's Command Card Crit. Rate and Crit. DMG, Shield Generation, HP Recovery, Aliemus, and Strength +[StateArg1]%. If the current realm is {Ultra}, gain [StateArg2] Keyflare after releasing {Annihilation}. After entering the {Ultra Round}, the next Posse is released twice this turn.
wheel-0035.lore:
When she opened her eyes in the greenhouse, the fire had already engulfed the entire garden, coming right before her.
The door of the greenhouse was locked from the outside, and the strong smell of alcohol and grease filled the air. She called out loudly for Colette's name until her voice was hoarse from the smoke, and all that responded to her was the roaring of the flames.
She felt relieved. Fortunately, Colette had gone out; otherwise, with such a large fire, she would also be in danger.
She felt sad. In her last moments of life, she couldn't see her best friend one last time. When Colette returned and saw the burned garden and herself, she would surely be very sad, right?
She felt regret. Their lives were just getting better, but this fire would put an end to them.
Burning. The house is burning. The sky is burning. The leaves are burning. The petals are burning. The scenes experienced in the garden play out and flow before her: she brought her to the garden, she handed her a rose, she brought her soft bread after a day's toil. They frolicked, chased, lay under the tranquil sunlight, dreaming of the day they would move into the Countess's room.
Burning. The past is burning. The future is burning. The garden, with all its stories and emotions, burns fiercely.
Tears slid down her face, quickly disappearing under the scorching flames.
She felt beauty. In poetry, pain and regret are beautiful. She searched around and found the paper and pen that had been almost entirely consumed by the flames.
Gratitude, affection, reluctance, blessing, beauty. She wanted to leave this most beautiful everything to Colette.
"...To Colette. Horla, in the fire."
At the end of the poem, she left her final signature.
A hundred meters away, on the road opposite the garden, Colette seemed to sense something. She looked up, the fierce flames reflecting on her face.
Tears slid down her face, quickly disappearing under the scorching flames.

wheel-0036.name: Path Forsaken
wheel-0036.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: After the wielder deals DMG, there is a [StateArg1]% chance to draw 1 of their Command Cards and reduce its Arithmetica Cost by 1. This effect activates only once per turn.
wheel-0036.lore:
The girl opens her eyes to find herself in darkness.
"Hey!" she shouts, "Is anyone there?"
The echo wavers and fades in the space with no visible boundaries as she takes a deep breath and shouts again, "Sister, Brown, Westie!"
"Is anyone there—"
A louder question than before drifts away and slowly returns. A few strands of bluish light seem to be drawn in by the echo from afar, formed by countless hovering, dust-like particles, winding through the air like serpents, rising and falling like waves. A continuous stream of light passes by the girl standing still, quickly turning the entire realm into a sea of stars, the tiny particles swishing through the girl's fingers and hair, leaving no trace.
"What is this...?"
The glowing dust undulates slightly around her, offering no response.
"I remember the wax museum, I remember my sister, I... seem to have died. Is this heaven?" She looks around, "Why is there not even an angel here? It can't be hell, can it!"
Her voice falls beside her ear, and this time, even the echo does not appear again.
An inexplicable sense of loneliness arises from deep within her heart, and in a daze, she feels as if she has returned to before she met her sister and Brown. The foul odor from the dark sewer permeates, she is cold and hungry, yet unable to leave because someone pushed her down and a large barrel pressed down on top of the sewer.
She slaps her face and steps forward.
The dust parts in front of her, then gathers behind her. Lift a leg, set it down, lift a leg, set it down. She keeps walking and walking, unsure how long she has been walking or how far. Ahead of her are more dust and darkness, and behind her, whether the direction she came from or the path she had walked, has already left no trace until —
"Awake succeeded, Jenkin, Jenkin, can you hear me?"

wheel-0037.name: Core Meltdown
wheel-0037.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: The Base DMG of the wielder's "Strike" +25%, and the Base Shield of "Defense" +25%. When playing the wielder's "Strike, " gain Temporary {STR} equal to the wielder's ATK [StateArg1]%, and when playing the wielder's "Defense, " gain Temporary {Alert} equal to the wielder's DEF [StateArg2]%. If the current realm is {Ultra}, receive a Temporary {STR} and Temporary {Alert} when entering the {Ultra Round}
wheel-0037.lore:
A.F.@1/@1/@2

08:00

Assemble parts numbered 107 to 133.

C-type components just need this final adjustment, and once completed, they'll be used as the energy core for the automaton "Tick-tock Man." 

I wanted to assemble it personally, but @2 said the supervisor needs to learn to trust their coworkers.

How shameless! It's obviously her who checks the parts until midnight.

-------

A.F.@1/@1/@2

00:21

The MF311000 motor has been assembled; the gears fit, and the pipes flow freely. Currently, all skills are functioning normally. Final adjustments are about to commence.

13:07

Special energy S has been connected. The MF311000 motor is running well and fits perfectly with the EA307850 artificial body, which is functioning normally.

@2 is quite optimistic. She thinks we can go farther this time than before. I reminded her not to forget what happened during the fifth awakening attempt, and she just plugged her ears and said she couldn't hear. If I hadn't reacted quickly then, the flames from the self-igniting energy core would have scorched her hair.

She really should experience the feeling of being bald—maybe that way, she'd learn to be cautious.

-------

A.F.@1/@1/@2

14:55

Final preparations completed. Energy ready. Containment device activated. Containment state: good. The torso will be installed after awakening is completed.

15:00

Commenced the 13th activation attempt of "Tick-tock Man." 

15:05

The torso EA307853 is operating normally, and the MF311000 motor is functioning correctly. The neck joint of "Tick-tock Man" is moving well, and the head components are operating normally.

15:15

"Tick-tock Man" successfully executed blinking, gaze-following, and mouth-opening commands. The motor exhibits a heating phenomenon, but it remains within acceptable limits.

Preparing to install the limbs. May the heavens bless us for success this time.

18:07

While executing walking commands, the motor core melted, and the circuits burned out. Special energy S remained intact.

@2 is very protective of this special energy. Even I am forbidden to touch it. I think I can understand—it truly is a mysterious object. It looks just like ordinary metallic silver and doesn't seem capable of energy storage, yet it can power the automaton, and such high temperatures haven't damaged it. It's simply incredible.

If this thing could be mass-produced or extensively mined...

Perhaps I should find a day to ask her again. I don't know how much longer she'll spend on this automaton. If one day she herself turns into an automaton, I wouldn't be surprised.

wheel-0038.name: Happy Magic Show
wheel-0038.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: After the wielder releases an Exalt, they gain [StateArg1] Aliemus and [StateArg2] Keyflare, repeating once for every 4 cards in hand. If the current Realm is {Ultra}, after the wielder's Cards enter {Ultra Space}, they gain Temporary {STR} equal to [StateArg3]% of the wielder's Attack, triggering up to 2 times per turn.
wheel-0038.lore:
"My masterpiece? Hmm—so curious, huh? Alright, alright, after all, it's a request from a VVIP. But a plain narration wouldn't suit my title as a master magician, so let me present it to you through a grand performance!
Come, come, please take your seat in the VIP area. The suspenseful magical memory show filled with money and betrayal is about to begin!"
-------
With a snap of her fingers, the lights came on, and Casiah's magic hat floated in mid-air. Its yellow opening opened and closed, surprisingly emitting Casiah's voice.
"Not long ago, in a not-so-distant place, there was a circus with rabbits, clowns, lions, and elephants—"
As the hat shook, out tumbled a rabbit, a clown, a lion, and an elephant. The drumbeat grew increasingly urgent, accompanied by the sound of cannon blasts. Casiah, dressed in splendid theatrical attire, emerged from the hat and placed it on her own head.
"Most importantly, there was a talented and gifted magician's apprentice!"
-------
The apprentice's growth was met with applause, even attracting a lavishly dressed investor. Strangely, ever since the investor joined them, rabbits, clowns, lions, elephants... many people and animals disappeared one by one. The stage emptied significantly, yet the apprentice seemed oblivious, repeating her former routine until the investor handed her a glass of wine.
The apprentice drank the wine, fainted, and the stage instantly went dark. Faint sighs drifted from the darkness.
"That was when I first understood regret."
-------
"But is regret enough? I want to know how it feels when the benefactor cries, how it feels when the audience laughs. I want to know more, much more. At that moment—
I opened my eyes."
Paper confetti exploded in the air, fragments fluttering down. Casiah suddenly appeared in the center of the stage. She tapped the brim of her hat, and the top hat opened wide, revealing a dazzling light.
"I opened my eyes and realized that all emotions are light, all can be stored in my magic hat.
Now, please relax, my dear VIP. The next segment won't hurt at all—it will only make you feel a bit numb, a little indifferent to life..."

wheel-0039.name: Unbearable Freedom
wheel-0039.descriptionTemplate:
Team Unique: Hand Limit +2. Effects of the same type cannot stack. The wielder's Base DMG and Shield Generation +[StateArg1]%.
Obtain [StateArg2] Aliemus upon entering the {Ultra Round}.
Release {Annihilation} to grant all Awakeners Temporary Crit. DMG +[StateArg3]%. This effect has a Cooldown of 3 turns.
wheel-0039.lore:
To this day, Castor cannot explain the true meaning of freedom.

He often feels like the more foolish one among the twins, clumsily following Pollux since his youth, listening to his brother's wild ideas one after another.

In the dull and numb life of training, he thought that having that touch of lightness was the happiest thing—until Juliette ordered the regular transformation ritual for Pollux.
