Endless pain was poured entirely onto his brother, while Castor could only stay helplessly by his side, watching the vibrant smile fade more and more from his brother's face, powerless to stop it.

"Do you know what freedom means?" In the dead of night, Pollux silently asked Castor, holding the worn manuscript they had both flipped through countless times.

Castor shook his head.

"Then, do you want to see the outside world, brother?"

Castor was stunned. He seemed to understand what Pollux wanted to pursue—those things he had never seen, existing only in the withered texts, beyond his imagination.

He was deeply shaken by such a rebellious and shocking idea. He remained silent, perhaps for a long time, or perhaps just a few breaths, then he nodded without fear.

Even if it meant going up against the entire church. Even if it meant sacrificing his life.

Thus, a pair of birds flew out of the cage they had never left. But unfortunately, they never thought the price of freedom would be so unbearable.

When the Hunter's gunfire sounded, the birds fell from the sky.

Castor had just begun to taste the flavor of freedom, yet he helplessly watched his brother's wings, which were carrying them toward it, being brutally torn out.

Faced with Juliette's terrifying anger, his pale and trembling brother showed him an unyielding smile from amidst the crowd.

"Hold on, for the sake of freedom."

Castor kept replaying Pollux's silent lips in his mind, until, in the dim torment, he chewed those two faith-like words into a burning brand. When he finally met Pollux again, he was astonished to find that the person who had always led him forward had heartlessly abandoned them.

wheel-0040.name: Eternal Requiem
wheel-0040.descriptionTemplate:
At the start of investigation, add 1 {Silver Key Dawn} with {Destroy} to the deck.
Whenever you unleash a Posse, the wielder's Crit. DMG +[StateArg1]%.
Whenever the wielder deals DMG, their Crit. Rate +[StateArg3]% this turn and you gain Keyflare equal to [StateArg2]% of their Keyflare Regen, up to 5 times each turn.
wheel-0040.lore:
He always pushed himself hard and drove away all his companions with his harshness, thinking he would walk this path alone forever in pursuit of musical perfection.
The heights are cold, and he accepted this destined fate.
Until he heard that person's performance.
It had nothing to do with perfection. The music was too unrestrained, like the person himself, sometimes deviating from the score and even suddenly forgetting parts. Then, his hands would improvise with clever, lively melodies, earning surprise and praise.
He watched him, as if watching a musical sprite play among the notes.
This is what his father called talent, indeed. He was deeply attracted, like everyone else, but he couldn't change his steps.
He was too ordinary and foolish, only able to traverse this laboriously paved path.
Everyone followed that person, and he grew further and further apart.
He longed to be close to him, but besides playing, he knew nothing else.
How to converse with him? What kind of score would attract his attention?
He composed scores as if drafting opening words. Until he had no chance to speak again.
Deep regret consumed him, making all else meaningless.
Only that unfinished letter remained, meant for the departed soul.
He exhausted himself, ultimately collapsing before the score, dreaming without reaching the end of the path.
If I can never reach the end, what is my life's persistence for?
Distant flute music from the light pacified his questioning soul.
In a daze, he picked up the flute, realizing his true mission.
With his ceaseless playing, bring eternal peace.

wheel-0041.name: Veiled Anguish
wheel-0041.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: The wielder's Exalt Base DMG +[StateArg1]%, and Keyflare Regen +[StateArg2]. After entering {Ultra Round}, all Awakeners obtain [StateArg4] Aliemus. After releasing {Annihilation}, the wielder obtains [StateArg3] Aliemus.
wheel-0041.lore:
Clementine was not a strong child, so when afflicted by a strange illness, tormented by pain and the looming death, she would often awake in the dead of night, crying silently.
And each morning after her cries, a "Gift" would appear at her bedside.
A few pieces of lavishly wrapped candy, trendy hair accessories, and adorably stuffed bears...
But what left the deepest impression was a specimen of a cocoon.
At that time, she was gravely ill, bedridden, struggling to lift up the specimen to gaze at the life within the light-pierced cocoon.
That life was sleeping, but that life had achieved eternity.
While she was nearing true death.
Thinking of this, her tears silently fell. But a pair of hands wiped her tears away.
"Don't cry, Clementine. Someday, you too will be reborn like these cocoons."
"I will give you new life."
Her father never lied, at least not to her, so Clementine had no doubt in his words.
She trusted her father, and so when she entered that profane mountain, she did so without hesitation, and when she endured the agonizing pain of dissolving flesh, she did so without complaint.
Because she believed all this was the price and necessary process for rebirth.
When all the pain and despair were over, she would heal, return to the place called "home," and reunite with her only family.

wheel-0042.name: Twisted Knight Ballad
wheel-0042.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: The wielder's cards gain +[StateArg1]% Base DMG and Crit. DMG. At the start of your turn and after using the wielder's Exalt, gain 1 stack of {Alight}. When the wielder's Command Card is played, consume 1 stack to increase its Final DMG and Shield by [StateArg2]%. During an {Ultra Round}, gain {Ablaze} instead (effect doubled).
wheel-0042.lore:
Pollux dreamed.


He was clad in silver armour, receiving a sword, smooth as moonlight, from the hands of a majestic king.

He mounted a tall white horse and galloped into a cavern, the trees at its mouth whispering as he plunged underground.


In the depths lurked a shadow, dark green and monstrous, a mountain of flesh with strange, glowing eyes.


"Creature that harms the kingdom," Pollux declared, "I cannot suffer you to threaten our sacred realm. Today, this blade of justice will end you."


The monstrous mountain shuddered, answering with a terrifying roar.

Pollux did not understand its language, nor did he care to.

He raised the sword and fought the evil-tinged thing.

Its tentacles writhed in the dimness, clashing against the radiant blade with a sound like a cracked bell.


And as in every hero's tale, the brave knight mounted the creature's back and, with a fierce cry, drove the sword deep into its chest.


The monster collapsed.

Pollux, breathless, stared at the mark spreading beneath the blade.

How could such a warm, bright red blood flow from something so ugly and evil?


He woke suddenly. The sticky sensation of blood on his hands seemed impossible to scrub away.

From the corner of his eye, he saw the old book Castor cherished, hidden in a gap behind the bed.


He flipped through it idly, the pages falling open to the knight's tale Castor loved so.

There, in the centre of the page, was a prophecy:


*When the evil monster is defeated by the warrior, all will see a vision in the sky; the sun will turn to darkness, and the moon to blood.*


Pollux frowned and tossed the book back into its hiding place with disdain.

Beneath the moonlight, he murmured a prayer.


"The Light's servant is sent here to bless you, to cleanse you of your sins, to return from darkness to light, and to join all pure and righteous souls in the promised land..."


But the Lightbearer offered no comfort to his devout squire.

The nightmare's unease still trembled in his heart, where his slumbering soul wept silently.

wheel-0043.name: Special Training
wheel-0043.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: After unleashing a Posse, the wielder's Crit. Rate is temporarily increased by [StateArg1]%.
wheel-0043.lore:
Even without the memories of your life before this point, your underdeveloped muscles clearly indicate that you were never the type to love sports or violence.

You really can't understand why Mythag set such high and comprehensive combat standards for Investigators.

Shouldn't the focus of an Investigator lie in the word "investigation"—in a sharp mind, rigorous logic, and a bit of well-timed luck?

Every time Ramona dragged you out of your warm bed in the dormitory on time, and you stood on the empty drill ground facing wave after wave of attacks, you missed Professor Doll's sarcastic logic classes more than ever. Even piles of homework and the most hair-raising exams were far gentler than the pain of being beaten and falling repeatedly during combat training.

On the 19th attempt, you lost your balance under the relentless pressure of the rapier that blocked all your escape routes and fell flat on your back, feeling as if every bone in your body was about to shatter. Gasping for breath, you wiped the sweat from your forehead in frustration and absolutely refused to get up again.

Ramona showed no surprise at all. She executed a graceful flourish with her sword and sheathed it, her heels clicking crisply as she walked toward you.

You shut your eyes tightly, determined to brace yourself for all kinds of scolding and criticism. Surprisingly, no blows or insults came. After three seconds, you opened your eyes again and saw only a faint smile right in front of you. Ramona slightly bent down, her gloved hand extended toward you.

"You've actually tried really hard, I know. But what we face is always the unknown fear and danger. Even I have moments of unexpected oversight."

"Just to make me feel a little more at ease, let's try one more time, partner."

wheel-0044.name: School Day
wheel-0044.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: After resetting stock at "D-Mark" and {Ashen Ruins}, the wielder gains [StateArg1] Aliemus.
wheel-0044.lore:
A year has passed since you first arrived at Mythag.

The memory of that initial day remains vivid, stepping into the halls of this ancient school still dazed from the aftermath of that cataclysmic explosion.

You were given a uniform that fit as though tailored by fate itself, and a dorm room that slowly became a sanctuary.

That first visit to the cafeteria, devouring two towering stacks of plates, feels both like yesterday and another lifetime.


Those effortless days ended abruptly.

Soon enough, it became clear that your knowledge was meager, your mind as untrained as a newborn's.

You were assigned to a year of preparatory classes: language drills, grueling physical training, Silver ability assessments, and intense lessons in special biology.

You endured it all, with mixed results.


You failed your Standard Leonian language class spectacularly, but you also achieved the extraordinary by linking with three Awakeners at once.

Overnight, you became something of a legend on campus.

Yet it was your raw, peculiar talent that ultimately saved you, propelling you past the threshold to become a full Investigator and sparing you another year in preparatory limbo.


Your weaknesses, jagged and undeniable, were slowly softened, covered by the growing trust and skill of your teammates.

Ah, your team; devils, perhaps, but reliable and gentle ones.


This place, often shrouded in mist and frequent rain, where sunlight is a rare gift, is home to countless souls; some forgotten, some soon to be.

Yet they persist.

They sacrifice.

They move forward.


On just such an ordinary, cloud-threaded day, Mythag welcomes another School Day.

You stand before the mirror, adjusting your collar, smoothing each wrinkle into place.

Then you push open the heavy door of the dormitory.


"Yawn.

Finally here.

I was almost asleep."

"What were you doing, hatching in your room?

Studying, freshman?"


You offer a sheepish chuckle to Lotan, shrink reflexively from the pink-clad figure that stirs a primal unease, and take shelter behind the calm presence of Ramona and Ogier, already smiling in quiet amusement.

Five shadows lengthen and sway in the pale morning light.


"Let's go, partner."

"From today, you are truly a part of Mythag."

wheel-0045.name: Dear Papa Noel
wheel-0045.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: Your first purchase of an "Orison" in the "D-Mark" store or your first purchase of a discounted item in the {Ashen Ruins} has a [StateArg1]% chance to cost 0 Black Sigils. This can trigger only once per Exploration.
wheel-0045.lore:
"O you who walk in lifelong penance, may this gift warm your soul, may the Father's love soothe your agony, and bring you sweet, peaceful dreams."

The silver-frosted night neared its end, and the candlelight flickered low.
For the first time in a long while, Salvador set aside the heavy stone tablets.
He exchanged his solemn, austere robes for a warm, crimson-velvet outfit.
In preparation for the coming Solstice Passage, he had visited Daffodil's shop to prepare holiday surprises for the students of Mythag.

"This is the last gift box..."
Salvador settled quietly onto an old chair in a corner of the campus.
Exhaustion finally overtook him, and he unconsciously drifted into a deep slumber.
It was a sweet dream.
The gift box at his feet mysteriously opened—four gingerbread girls leaped out, their frosting-dotted eyes shining brightly.
The scents of cinnamon and clove whirled in a circle in the cold air.
That fragrance stirred long-buried memories.
He found himself once more in the cathedral cloisters of his childhood, the melting toffee on his tongue sending quiet signals of sweetness.
All the blessings once tainted by suffering and plague now quietly awakened, twirling softly to the rhythm of this sugary waltz.

Meanwhile, four sneaky little figures crept toward the sleeping Salvador.
"Master... will Master think we are naughty...?"
"Hehe... Aurita's getting so, so curious! Hurry, open it! One, two... wow, so many gingerbread cookies!"
"You, you said your name out loud...! What if... people are gonna hate us...!"
"Then we can just push it on Brown! We're good girls! Didn't do anything!"

Salvador heard the little gingerbread girls chattering, and felt one of them hop onto his lap, the distinct scent of cinnamon brushing against his wrinkled hand.
He realized with a gentle jolt that these were not the phantom sweets of his dream, but the mischievous children of reality.
He could easily imagine their happy, glowing faces as they snuck open the gift box.
For this moment of beauty, happiness, and peace, he was willing to bear any pain.

"Oh... my beard, are they tying it to my hair?"

Salvador pretended to remain fast asleep, allowing the children to continue their joyful "secret mission."

wheel-0046.name: Countdown Moment
wheel-0046.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: At the start of exploration, add 1 card "5: Countdown" to the deck. "5: Countdown": Draw 1 card after played, shuffle the next "Countdown" into the Discard Pile, {Destroy}. "0: Countdown": Draw 3 cards, gain 3 Arithmetica, and have a [StateArg1]% chance to shuffle "5: Countdown" into the Discard Pile, {Retain}, {Destroy}.
wheel-0046.lore:
In the new year, you decide to hold an unprecedented ceremony to solemnly welcome the passage of time.
To this end, you have been preparing for a long time. You light candles, letting their warm glow illuminate the winter night on campus; you set up a high platform in the center of the training ground, covered with a deep red velvet cloth adorned with intricate patterns. you even cut countless colorful ribbons wrapped in gold foil and silver gauze by hand, hoping that this countdown moment transcends the melancholy of farewell, transforming into an everlasting mark embraced by the year-end and the new year.
But... something is still missing.
You walk deep into the old warehouse, brushing away layers of dust, and finally find that antique clock. Its shell is worn, the hands have long since broken and fallen due to the erosion of time, and it is clearly unusable.
Just when you are at your wits end, Thais appears, quietly standing behind a brand new clock.
That clock feels incredibly familiar. She still smiles playfully, but there is a certain gentle certainty hidden in her eyes.
"What do you think? This clock, paired with my dance, will surely be the grandest ceremony."
"This... can't be Tavi's clock, can it?"
"Oh, you know she has been all alone in the pool, it's too dull for her."
"Letting her join our ceremony and feel my love might spark her interest a bit~"
Midnight quietly arrives, and Thais stands before the giant clock, like a gft wrapped by time itself.
The ribbons swirl around her body like unburned fireworks, creating golden-red and silver-blue ripples in the flowing light. The plump bow at her waist is the last knot tied by the new year—she is the grandest gift of the year-end, wrapped in joyful satin, waiting to be unsealed by time.
Her arms slowly rise, her fingertips tracing the light and shadow, ultimately pointing to the hanging clock behind her. Twelve numbers connect in a halo on the clock face, dancing at her fingertips.
The congregation gazes at Thais's exuberant dance, seemingly infected by the joyous atmostphere, beginning to sway in rhythm with her.
And as the chime falls, she lightly taps with her fingertip, touching the beginning of eternity. The bound gift opens its arms, transforming all the celebration stored at year-end into a shower of blessings that scatter across the world.

wheel-0047.name: Stakes of Wisdom
wheel-0047.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: After exploration starts, shuffle 1 {Stakes of Wisdom} into the Draw Pile. {Stakes of Wisdom}: Cost 1 Arithmetica. Shuffle all Command Cards in hand randomly into the Draw Pile. Shuffled Command Cards have a [StateArg1]% chance to take effect 1 additional time when played next, and for each Command Card shuffled in, all Awakeners gain 2 Aliemus. {Retain}. {Exhaust}. {Innate}.
wheel-0047.lore:
At the entrance of a lavishly gilded club, a doorman in a crisp white shirt raises a hand, politely barring Ryker's path.

"Sir, you possess no invitation and are not a member. I'm afraid entry is impossible."

"Is that so? Quite the predicament..."

Ryker strokes his chin, putting on a show of deep thought. In truth, he is simply biding his time. He is waiting for the suspect in the case at hand. He is waiting for that destined blessing of fate.

Sure enough, his patience pays off.

"If it isn't the Lucky Detective! Londinium's finest," a voice calls out. "Come, I'd be more than happy to vouch for you."

A gentleman steps forward, a gracious smile on his face that doesn't quite reach his eyes. "I've long wanted to witness that legendary luck of yours. Please, don't disappoint me..."

The look in the man's eyes is laced with pure malice. Such expressions are common in this club; one can hardly expect gamblers chasing a high to play by the rules. When Ryker realizes he is being set up, he doesn't mind in the least. He simply curls the corner of his mouth and calmly picks up his whist cards.

No need to rush—the game has only just begun, hasn't it?

The first round passes, then the second, then the third. One game follows another until Ryker has lost every last chip.

"Lucky Detective, it seems your luck has finally deserted you," the gentleman sneers. "Then again, no one can be blessed by Fate forever." He leans over the table, his gaze hungry. "Care to continue? It's a shame, Detective—you have nothing left."

"Nothing left? I wouldn't say that."

The detective, who has lost every single round, smiles and gestures to himself. "I still have this fine suit, this expensive cane, and..."

He pauses, drawing a small, compact device from his breast pocket. He smiles, turning it over in his fingers as he lowers his voice. "I'll admit your cheating methods are remarkably clever. Even the owner of this club couldn't crack them; he had to seek my help. But even the most ingenious scheme takes time, requires execution, and demands coordination."

He taps the device. "As luck would have it, this little gadget from Mythag has already clearly recorded the evidence of you and your accomplices cheating in this club just now."

The gentleman freezes. His expression shifts from shock to a simmering, murderous fury. His accomplices are even more incensed, itching to teach the detective a lesson. But with so many eyes watching, the gentleman doesn't dare make a rash move—and besides, the detective has offered him a choice he cannot refuse.

"How about one more wager?" Ryker asks. "If you win, this little device—and my life—will be yours. You know what I mean."

"And if I lose?"

"Then I'm afraid you'll have to turn yourself in." Ryker gives a casual shrug, flashing a gleaming grin. "Do you really think you'll lose? A true gambler only ever believes they'll win."

Indeed, every gambler believes the next hand is theirs. No one in this room could refuse such a wager.

The whist cards flow across the table once more—playing, covering, seizing—until, at last, a winner is decided.

"Lucky~"

Without sparing the loser another glance, Ryker laughs and flings his winnings into the air with a wave of his hand. The already boisterous club erupts into total fervor. The air fills with the cries of people scrambling for coins, the shrieks of those claiming their windfalls, and peals of wild laughter.

Amid the chaos, Ryker slips the Mythag-issued decorative device—a standard trinket anyone can buy—back into his breast pocket. He pats the invitation bearing his own name in his trouser pocket, curls his lip, and presses down the brim of his hat.

A true gambler only ever believes they will win.

Because he is certain that no matter what he does, the blessing of fate will always walk beside him.

wheel-0048.name: Unsent Flowers
wheel-0048.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: If the wielder is the Awakener "Ramona," the Affinity gained upon completing the stage +[StateArg1]%.
wheel-0048.lore:
A bouquet that has withered and dried from being left for too long.
It will never see its master return.

wheel-0049.name: Roses in Mud
wheel-0049.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: If the wielder is the Awakener "Ramona: Timeworn," the Affinity gained by other Awakeners upon completing the stage +[StateArg1]%, but the wielder does not gain any Affinity.
wheel-0049.lore:
In a loop that cannot be recorded, Ramona: Timeworn steps over the flowerbed behind the clock tower.
Out of the corner of her eye, she catches sight of the rose garden, where clusters of vibrant flowers are blooming fiercely.
After a rare moment of pause, she casts aside all unnecessary thoughts and continues her journey to the final battlefield.
The mud splashes onto the crimson petals, quickly devoured by the dark D-Slime, leaving only a proud silhouette in the wind.

wheel-0050.name: Hymn of the Sovereign
wheel-0050.descriptionTemplate:
At the start of each battle, the wielder gains [StateArg1] Aliemus.
Whenever another Awakener Exalts, the wielder gains [StateArg2] Aliemus.
After the wielder Exalts, gain {Tentacle DMG} equal to [StateArg3]% of their ATK.
wheel-0050.lore:
He is the king who knows all, the supreme ruler of Lemuria.

Lapis Lazuli serves as his throne, gold as his crown. Souls are his scepter, and he rests at the pinnacle.

Priests who serve the gods also serve him, kneeling before him,

loyal subjects and devout believers.


At night, the gods send moonlight, inviting him to the eternal palace,

where they share the food of souls, bestowing upon him divine wisdom.

Endless dreams allow him to witness the beginning of all things and the end of time.

He has learned: All beginnings stem from chaos; the gods awoke and built floating palaces in the darkness.

He has learned: The end of time is darkness, and all returns to chaos.


King of kings, beloved of the gods,

Who can compare to you?

Who commands in dreams as you do, wielding your majesty?

Noble god-king, youthful sovereign,

Sleep soundly.

Your subjects and followers will patiently await your return, bringing back the prophecy of the gods.

Respected ruler of the Nereids, master of the abyss,

Let us call upon your name and grant us protection,

Tulu, Tulu.

wheel-0051.name: Shrouded Birth
wheel-0051.descriptionTemplate:
Shield gained from the wielder +[StateArg1]%.
Whenever the wielder or their equipment provide Shield, all Awakeners gain +[StateArg3]% Temp. Crit. Rate, and you gain Keyflare equal to [StateArg4]% of the wielder's Keyflare Regen, up to 2 times per turn.
Whenever a Tentacle attacks, the wielder gains 1 Aliemus (up to [StateArg2] per turn).
wheel-0051.lore:
Mother. Mother.
Murphy has lost most of the memories related to her.
It is said that her dying mother protected her, blood choking in her throat.
Then people rushed forward, rudely separating them.
In that moment, Murphy saw her mother's face. That face was frantic, yet very beautiful.
After she began to remember, she had a new "mother".
Gentle and cautious, she would fulfill all of Murphy's unreasonable demands while maintaining a distance that was neither too far nor too close.
Just as Murphy thought their relationship would continue like this forever, like Miryam's unchanging expression, she was lifted high upon the dark Throne and saw the glowing "Egg" in her dreams.
It sparkled in the dark night, like an eye or a star. Ethereal songs surrounded the sanctuary:
<Italic:From the @1 of her daughter, the God descends @1...>
She was given a great mission, both related and unrelated to her: to become the mother who brings the God back to life.
How strange. She still does not understand what a mother is, what a daughter is, yet she must begin to learn to be a "mother".
Murphy recalled that frantic, beautiful face, and the sweet yet bitter taste on her tongue.
That beautiful person does not want me to fall into the grave like this, right?

wheel-0052.name: Amidst the Downpour
wheel-0052.descriptionTemplate:
The wielder's Strike Commands have a [StateArg1]% chance to gain 1 Arithmetica and trigger 10% of each enemy's {Poison}. This effect triggers only once each turn.
If the Realm includes {Aequor}, this effect also triggers 1 Tentacle attack.
wheel-0052.lore:
My boat was tightly entwined by something; the propeller struggled strenuously, making terrifying sounds.
Cumulonimbus clouds gathered above us, with lightning surging through the dark clouds.
The moment a thunderbolt struck the mast, the propeller broke free and started spinning again.
I sighed with relief, but the chief engineer began to scream.

A viscous liquid mixed into the seawater, and in the wild storm outside, the smell was carried away by the wind, leaving only the scent of death in my nose.
But I realized that the glowing liquid was some kind of blood.
My dearest companion, my most trusted ship, gave her all before sinking—her blades, which should have sliced through the water, had severed the monster's tentacles.

This is a good omen! We'll soon be out of the storm!
I was about to comfort my crew with this thought when, as I looked up, a light appeared in the center of the dark sea.
A lighthouse? How could it be? No, impossible.
We're in the middle of the ocean. The nearest land is hundreds of miles away. How could anyone build a lighthouse here?

My sailors began to cry, pray, and sing songs of home in unison.
Then, the lighthouse abruptly went out.
All light vanished, and the roaring storm turned into a drizzle on the deck.
The sailors praised the merciful Lord, immersed in the joy of surviving the ordeal.
But I saw it clearly.

The figure that disappeared in the light was indeed a graceful woman.

wheel-0053.name: Fin of Sorrow
wheel-0053.descriptionTemplate:
Shield gained from and HP recovered by the wielder each increase by [StateArg1]% of their CON, and {Counter} gained from them +[StateArg3]%.
Whenever one of the wielder's Defense Commands is played, gain {Counter} equal to [StateArg4]% of their DEF.
At the start of battle, gain {STR} and {Tentacle DMG} equal to [StateArg2]% of the wielder's CON.
wheel-0053.lore:
(The following answers are excerpted from Dr. Clementine's diagnostic manual for reference only)


1. Caecus.

2. 16, 17...? Who knows.
Why should I care? I'm already dead.

3. A rural fishing village, you wouldn't find its name on any map.

4. I don't want to say.

5. Freak. Ugly. Silly pig. Stupid @1... these are rather common.

6. Shimmering golden catfish face, abbreviated as "catfish face".

7. Rainy days.

8. I don't know. When it rains, I walk back and forth on the beach...
It's been a few years. I just look in the distance, nothing else.

9. I SAID I DON'T #¥% KNOW, @1 GET LOST!!
-------

14. Umm... maybe it's because, Uncle Ornn once told me he would come take me "home" in a big sailboat when it rained...

15. Yes, he fulfilled his promise...
I drifted at the bottom of the sea for several days, starving and thirsty, every part of my body in pain...
Then I saw Uncle Ornn and his white sailboat.
His whole body... No, his whole skeleton, to be precise, was hanging on the mast of the sailboat—he also failed his transformation.
Then, with my last strength, I drifted over and sat on the mast with him.

16. We promised to go home together.

wheel-0054.name: Dream of the Sea
wheel-0054.descriptionTemplate:
Whenever one of the wielder's cards is played, they gain 1 Aliemus.
At the start of battle, gain {STR} equal to [StateArg1]% of the wielder's ATK and {Tentacle DMG} equal to [StateArg2]% of their ATK.
At the start of each following turn, gain {STR} equal to [StateArg3]% of the wielder's ATK and {Tentacle DMG} equal to [StateArg4]% of their ATK.
wheel-0054.lore:
When Aurita was born, the impact of the great flood on the city had completely faded, but in the remote towns, people were still struggling to survive.

The flood made people begin to fear the sea and rivers; those who could leave had already departed, and those who remain in this small fishing village are mostly women and children who find it difficult to establish themselves elsewhere.
In this dull and quiet place, Aurita is the only child.

She stubbornly believes in the fairy tales her grandmother told her—that her parents have returned to the sea, waiting for Aurita to grow up. Once she grows up, they will all be reunited and become a happy family again.

When she was still unable to speak, it felt as if she had once slept in a warm embrace, but Aurita can no longer remember many things about her parents.
However, as a child who grew up in a silent place, Aurita does not understand "loneliness" or "desolation"; she has her own group of friends.

Unlike those adults who were terrified by the flood, Aurita has never feared the sea.
As a resident of the Ocean King's domain, she naturally has to befriend the sea.
Aurita learns to play with the "locals".
She mimics the way clams move and dances, learning from clownfish as they swim through the seagrass.
She lies on the shellback of a leatherback turtle, drifting away from the land with the waves.
In the sea, bathed in moonlight, she plays with a swarm of jellyfish.

She is joy, she is imagination, she is a sprite.

She is a dream.

wheel-0055.name: Blade of the Titan
wheel-0055.descriptionTemplate: After the wielder Exalts, their Crit. DMG +[StateArg1]% this turn, and each of their cards in your hand has a [StateArg2]% chance to have their Arithmetica Cost reduced by 1 until the end of the turn.
wheel-0055.lore:
Separate. Experience. Flood in. Invade.

Scholars have long debated the true meaning of the Semitic root "p-l-sh" among the Perishites.
For the first time in his life, Goliath felt that these old scholars possessed some genuine insight, for these four brief words almost encapsulated his life as a Perishite.

The Perishites are indeed a maritime people.
They are like schools of long-tailed sharks, drawn to inhabitable islands by the scent of blood, then slaughtering all the natives there.

The Perishites never tire.
They never linger; when everything settles, their restless instincts drive them to move on, seeking new prey.

The Perishites don't care about tomorrow.
They squander their plundered wealth at an astonishing speed, drinking the last drop of wine, then breeding, fighting, and betraying with reckless abandon...

As they set sail, Goliath looked back at the trail left by the ships on the sea.
The blood of the Perishites, the blood of the natives, the blood of enemies, the blood of seabirds...
It merged into one, like dark red ink spilling into an endless blue.

The next day, the sun rose as usual, the sea shimmered like countless shards of broken gold, and everything vanished without a trace.
They were in the ocean, yet the ocean had forgotten them.

wheel-0056.name: Power of the Pious
wheel-0056.descriptionTemplate:
{Poison} inflicted by the wielder +[StateArg2]%, and Aliemus generated by the wielder +[StateArg1]%.
The first time the wielder deals DMG each turn, all Awakeners gain Temporary Crit. DMG equal to [StateArg3]% of the wielder's Crit. DMG.
If the Realm includes {Aequor}, Tentacle Limit +1, and spawn 1 Tentacle at the start of battle.
wheel-0056.lore:
O Master of Lemuria, hear my prayer.

Grant me your prophecy, bestow your insight,

So I may restore your kingdom.

In this faithless land, upon this ice-field ringed by sea,

Erect your pillars, and build your temple.


O Master of Abyss, receive these offerings.

Pure flesh from a full-moon lamb, a strong heart from a grown bull,

Sharp eyes from a soaring eagle, and hard bones from a cruising whale.

Lapis is your throne, gold your crown,

Grant me your dream, tell me your divine words,

So I may restore your form.

In the abyss under the ice, in the realm beneath the waves,

I offer the mother born at the right time.


O All-knowing King of Lemuria, please answer my prayer.

Centuries have passed since you descended into your long slumber.

In your name I act, and your glory I proclaim, never ceasing.

My faith is firm, my heart devout.

You are the only Godking, the embodiment of immortality.

May you end my wait, return to the world, and let me hear your words of wisdom.

wheel-0057.name: Death of Ms. Coral
wheel-0057.descriptionTemplate:
Whenever one of the wielder's cards is played, gain Shield equal to [StateArg1]% of the wielder's DEF, plus another [StateArg2]% of their DEF for each Tentacle.
This effect triggers only once each turn.
wheel-0057.lore:
She never dreams.

The young apprentice sculptor firmly believes in absolute rationality and order, just like her cold, lifeless works.
From the moment she first laid eyes on it, she was captivated.
Soft, pure—it was the perfect coloring material.
Under the moonlight, she worked tirelessly, bringing the black goo from the graveyard back to her studio.

A week later, a sculpture titled "@2" was exhibited at the fair.
That night, she heard waves from afar.
For the first time, she dreamed.
Under the tranquil morning light, the dust-covered underwater palace lay peacefully asleep.
She wandered through the white coral clusters, breathing in an atmosphere thick with loneliness and forgetfulness.

When she awoke, her fingers were curling, turning black, and eventually sprouted white coral.
She inhaled repeatedly, sensing the murmurs of ancient times, the countless whispers of long-departed creatures.
It was a truly soft fossil.
Nervously, she began to laugh—so this is what dreaming feels like.
She locked herself in the studio, starting to create obsessively.
Molding, sculpting, coloring...
Chaotic yet orderly. Inorganic yet organic.

She will forever dream.

wheel-0058.name: Sail of Providence
wheel-0058.descriptionTemplate:
HP recovered by the wielder +[StateArg1]%.
When the wielder Exalts, recover [StateArg2]% of missing HP at the start of your next turn.
wheel-0058.lore:
In Cathuria, legends tell of a sunken civilization.

It is said that before recorded history, an abnormal shift in the stars caused a prehistoric civilization to sink beneath the sea, and the Cathurians are its descendants.
Aside from trading and fishing, one major reason Cathurians venture into the sea is to search for their lost ancestral civilization.
They believe that girls born under specific star alignments can sense this ancient civilization, guiding them to its location and protecting the fleet from storms.

Celeste was chosen for this purpose.
Bound to the mast of a ship with white sails, she watched the people below bustling about.
She felt neither interest nor curiosity.

Celeste could sense the weather at sea through the wind and birds, and sometimes even catch a faint trace of the distant sunken land.
She felt as though she belonged to the sea and the ship—often thinking that her captivity did not stir any emotion within her.
Sometimes she would leave the mast, chasing the white birds flying across the sky, with the ship closely following her.
The people believed that the girl symbolized safe voyages, bringing blessings to ships so they could return safely laden with wealth and advanced knowledge.
But she knew nothing of this.

wheel-0059.name: Flamebound Navigator
wheel-0059.descriptionTemplate:
Shield gained from the wielder's Exalt +[StateArg1]%.
After the wielder Exalts, all Awakeners gain +[StateArg2]% Temp. Crit. DMG, and another +1% for each Tentacle.
The first time {Death Resistance} triggers each investigation, recover [StateArg3]% of Max HP at the start of your next turn.
wheel-0059.lore:
"With mundane copper and iron, we measure the trajectories of stars, but the endless abyss has its own algorithm, ever-changing."

In the gradually sinking cruise ship, Corposant recited the "Elegy of the Coxwain."

She had long been aware of her fate and had already chosen her destiny; thus, facing death, she felt no fear, only calmness—calmly burning, calmly completing her last navigation.

But the burning flames were too intense; she could not see the way ahead, could not make a sound, nor wave a flag. In such imperfect guidance, could the ship navigate correctly?

She did not know, and thus felt fear and worry.

At this moment, the "Elegy of the Coxwain" offered guidance: "Young navigator, you shall be embraced by the brine, becoming the guiding phosphorescence."

Guiding phosphorescence? Corposant fell silent. She had indeed heard the tales of St. Elmo's Fire among sailors, but how could a mere mortal body transform into the sacred light illuminating the world?

Yet even with hope fading, Corposant continuously prayed within her heart: praying that she could fulfill her duty, completing the final guidance.

No one could hear this prayer except for Corposant herself, but that was enough—

In the darkness, the Silver core, nourished by Dissolution slime, burst forth with intense light, fulfilling her final wish.

"The Tidal Black Book will record your name, causing the raging waves to compose an eternal requiem for you."

Ultimately, she died to the sea as foretold in the "Elegy of the Coxwain."

But she transcended the "Elegy of the Coxwain."

That was the arrogance and counsel of human navigators when facing the sea, yet now, she had become St. Elmo's Fire, eternally guiding the ships.

wheel-0060.name: The Faraway Eden
wheel-0060.descriptionTemplate:
At the start of each battle, the wielder gains Aliemus equal to [StateArg1]% of their Base Aliemus.
The Final DMG dealt by the wielder's Exalt increases by a percentage equal to [StateArg2]% of their Base Aliemus.
When you switch to the {Raging Waves} stance, gain Temp. Realm Mastery equal to [StateArg3]% of current Realm Mastery. This effect has a 3-turn cooldown.
wheel-0060.lore:
This is an island marked on no nautical chart.
Since its birth, the virus known as "civilization" has never set foot upon its pristine shores.
There is only sand, jungle, an endless stretch of sea... and one small girl.
.........
"Papa! Mama! Fish!"
"Whistle—"
Two dolphins glide leisurely to the girl's side, their cries melodic and clear.
One lifts her onto its back; the other takes the stunned fish from her hands and swallows it in a single gulp.
"Whistle—"
"Hehe. Vortice! Strong!"
Like a child untouched by the world, the girl flexes her small arms, beaming at the praise from "Papa" and "Mama."
"Today, sun! Good!"
"Vortice! Papa! Mama! Swim!"
At her words, the dolphin carrying her lets out a soft trill and begins a slow circle around the little island.
Warm sunlight spills over the girl, dispelling the damp chill that has lingered for days. A gentle breeze rustles through her hair, carrying the ocean's lazy breath.
Laughter, splashing, playful cries. On this isolated isle, there are no burdens of the modern world—only the most primal, purest joy.
As always, the two creatures and one girl spend another simple, carefree day.
.........
Until the endless calling rises up and devours that tranquility whole.
"Come back... Come back..."
"@1 needs you... @2...@4... needs you..."

wheel-0061.name: Gluttony

In {Astral Reign}, this effect changes to "Increases the chance for the wielder's Dimensional Image Relic to appear by 100%."
wheel-0062.lore: Neither dwell on the past nor yearn for the future.

wheel-0063.name: Favor

wheel-0063.lore: Offer love and death to the world.

wheel-0064.lore: After a great many years of amassing tremendous power, we hereby declare—

wheel-0067.name: Hope

In {Astral Reign}, this effect changes to "Increases the chance for the wielder's Dimensional Image Relic to appear by 100%."
wheel-0067.lore: Blessings for the birds and the bees.

wheel-0068.name: Epiphany

wheel-0068.lore: See that which cannot be seen; hear that which cannot be heard.

wheel-0069.name: Punishment

In the {Astral Reign}, the effect changes to "Increases the chance for the wielder's Dimensional Image Relic to appear by 100%."

wheel-0070.name: Blessing
wheel-0070.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: After a normal battle ends, the number of available {Orisons} becomes 4. {Orisons} on the wielder's cards has a [StateArg1]% chance to upgrade to Advanced. In {Astral Reign}, this effect changes to "Increases the chance for the wielder's Dimensional Image Relic to appear by 100%."

wheel-0071.name: Vitality
wheel-0071.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: Rest Areas provide an additional option: {Destroy} up to 3 Symptom cards, obtain [StateArg1] Black Sigils. In {Astral Reign}, this effect changes to "Increases the chance for the wielder's Dimensional Image Relic to appear by 100%."
wheel-0071.lore: On the brink of death, the will to live ignites.

wheel-0072.name: Exile
wheel-0072.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: Symptom Cards can be sold for [StateArg1] Black Sigils. In {Astral Reign}, this effect changes to "Increases the chance for the wielder's Dimensional Image Relic to appear by 100%.".

wheel-0073.name: Suction
wheel-0073.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: Increases the HP Recovery at Rest Areas by [StateArg1]%. After choosing to recover HP, add 2 "Insight" cards to the deck. In {Astral Reign}, this effect changes to "Increases the chance for the wielder's Dimensional Image Relic to appear by 100%."

wheel-0074.name: Aged
wheel-0074.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: After each battle ends, gain [StateArg1] Keyflare. In {Astral Reign}, this effect changes to "Increases the chance for the wielder's Dimensional Image Relic to appear by 100%."
wheel-0074.lore: We are as hollow as the scarecrows.

wheel-0076.name: Insight
wheel-0076.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: Vision +1 during exploration, gain [StateArg1] Black Sigil after the map is fully revealed. At the start of Boss Battles, {Weakness} and {Vulnerable} all enemies for 1 turn. In the {Astral Reign}, the effect is changed to "Increases the chance for the wielder's Dimensional Image Relic to appear by 100%."

wheel-0077.name: Duty's Gravitas
wheel-0077.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: After the wielder triggers Exalt, all allies gain [StateArg1] Aliemus.
wheel-0077.lore:
The acting dean is an oddity on the Mythag campus.

Almost each Mythag investigator has unique abilities, while she is one of the very few with no powers beyond ordinary human limits.

Yet she holds this vital role.

Born into an ordinary family in the Elke Kingdom, she grew fierce and tenacious, shaped by local traditions.

Later, her fellow prisoner in the camp, Sophia, ignited her passion for academic research.

Her remarkable academic achievements made her the most trusted student of her mentor, the former dean of the Mythag University Biological Institute.

Thus, after her mentor disappeared, she naturally stepped in as acting dean.

Under her steadfast leadership, the institute's research continues to run smoothly.

"Fear no violence, fear no power. And so, fear no duty."

wheel-0078.name: Elevated Focus
wheel-0078.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: After triggering Posse, the wielder gains [StateArg1] Aliemus.
wheel-0078.lore:
There is a custom among investigators:
On the night following the Keygrave Ceremony, senior investigators gather newcomers in the dormitory backyard.

They light candles and sit in a circle around their Silver Keys.

The seniors then share the unique story behind the uniquely numbered key they now hold.

Such gatherings always end up turning into storytelling sessions, and the two keys representing the "first generation," I-0001 and K-0001, naturally become the focus of conversation.


Few people know that K-0001, the Silver Key crafted by its owner, the first Keeper, now rests underground with him.

There, in timeless dark, it listens to his whispers for someone long departed.

"You know he's not really here, Alistair," says Doll.
"It might be made from your teacher's Silver Core, but it's not his soul."

"It's just a way to focus the spirit, Doll," Alistair smiled.
"Don't worry, I'm stable. I can't break down before our new Keeper grows up, right?"

wheel-0079.name: Cursed Binding
wheel-0079.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: The wielder's {Poison} inflicted +[StateArg1]%. "Strike" deals DMG equal to [StateArg2]% of the attacker's ATK as {Poison}. This effect can trigger a maximum of 3 times per turn.
wheel-0079.lore:
The first half of his life was chaotic, dark, and bloody.

Eat. Sleep. Repeat.
Then he opened his eyes and gained awareness.

They say he devoured all his siblings and became the sole survivor.

They say he is the offspring of the Great Serpent and a human.

A black, cursed collar encircles his neck.
It keeps him alive, controls him, and ensures he can never defy the Church.

He is full of questions, curious about the world, yet at the very moment of his birth, he was stripped of all other possibilities.

Beautiful, dangerous.
Secretive, flamboyant.
He is all of these.

Sadly, these barely scratch the surface of his essence.

Any pity you feel for Albino because of this account will lead you to ruin.

wheel-0080.name: Sever and Scar
wheel-0080.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: The wielder's "Strike" causes the target enemy to lose Temporary {STR} equal to [StateArg1]% the wielder's ATK. This effect can trigger a maximum of 3 times per turn.
wheel-0080.lore:
A doctor's duty is to heal.

But there happens to be a treatment that demands the sharpest blade: to cut through skin, pierce muscle, and open the chest.
It requires removing the rot, and then trusting the body to heal on its own.

This should not count as her sin.
Yet after every surgery, she would find herself telling the girl some stories, as if to make up for it.

She wasn't a good storyteller; her words were fragmented, disjointed.
Still, the girl listened intently.

Where her hands once cut, the flesh seems to draw strength from those words, slowly and stubbornly mending itself...

wheel-0081.name: Cloaked in the Night
wheel-0081.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: The wielder's {Counter} +[StateArg1]%. "Defense" grants Temporary {Counter} equal to the wielder's DEF ×[StateArg2]%. In Boss Battles, this becomes 3 times the Temporary {Counter}. After {Death Resistance} triggers, deals {Fixed DMG} equal to the wielder's {Counter} stack count [StateArg3]% to all enemies.
wheel-0081.lore:
The giant beast lurks in the night.

Inside the cabin, a mother wails for her lost daughter, finally breaking through the restraint she held all day.

Every midnight, her grief bursts forth, raw and unhidden.

It begins with sobs and rises to howls, then comes the calling of the name that no longer belongs to the beast.

The agony in her voice pierces the beast's heart, a heart that remains entirely human.

<Italic:Come home, Lisa.>
<Italic:Oh, my Lisa.>

The beast remains silent.

It curls up in the darkness and falls into a deep sleep.

One day, it will return—to the place called "home".

wheel-0082.name: Adventurer's Pack
wheel-0082.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: After playing the wielder's "Defense," gain Temporary {STR} equal to [StateArg1]% the wielder's ATK.
wheel-0082.lore:
No one is born a captain; at least Elton wasn't.

Before becoming a captain, he drifted between the navy and merchant ships, nearly turning pirate more than once.

Fortunately, he finally settled aboard an exploration vessel.
The ship sailed around the polar continent, each landing a new adventure.

His job was to carry the gears for the frail scientists.
He was proud of it: what he carried might be the keys to changing the world.
And the scientists might be the ones to rewrite history.

That was what Elton believed—until an ice storm trapped them in a cave, and until a band of cultists attacked them under the cover of night.

wheel-0083.name: Analysis of Death
wheel-0083.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: After killing an enemy, the wielder gains [StateArg1] Aliemus and 1% Crit. DMG, effective for the entire exploration.
wheel-0083.lore:
He'd always been a quiet, diligent student.

At 8:00 a.m., when other researchers hurried in with bread stuffed in their mouths, he had already finished a lab report.

He would stay until midnight, when the last light in the lab building was out, then slowly pack up and leave.

He also fed the nearby stray cats every day.

Later, the stray cats vanished—no one knew where they had gone.

Professor Crowley reviewed his lab notes and reports.
They were concise, meticulous—unremarkable, yet flawless.

This student puzzled him.

Puzzlement is good; it often leads to curiosity.

Of course, Crowley did not realize then: it could also lead to something else—death.

wheel-0084.name: To My Dearest Friend
wheel-0084.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: After the wielder uses Exalt, all allies' Crit. Rate + [StateArg1]% this turn.
wheel-0084.lore:
"You've been gone so long, and I didn't even notice.

I still keep dreaming of that day—the gunshot pain in my left arm, the blood loss haze, the frenzied rats chasing us...
Then you appeared, in your Mythag uniform, getting me out of that cursed old castle.

At the dream's end, you stood beside my sickbed and told me:
for investigators, death is always close behind.

I can no longer tell reality from dream.

Whenever I am confused, I reach for my left arm—if I feel the cold, intricate metal, I know I'm awake.

But most times, I grasp nothing but air."

wheel-0085.name: Memory Syndrome
wheel-0085.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: After playing a Symptom card, the wielder gains [StateArg1] Aliemus. This effect triggers up to 2 times per turn. After playing a Command Card, the wielder gains 1 Aliemus.
wheel-0085.lore:
Crowley held the title of "Most Popular Mentor" every year he taught.

His slightly awkward West Constante accent and genuine smile won over countless students, including Herbert.

In A.F.2@1, Crowley defied objections and took Herbert, who was unremarkable at that time, as his student.

Together, they crossed mountains, forded streams, and trekked deserts, leaving footprints outside one D-Rift after another.


Herbert can still remember the shock of seeing a D-Rift for the first time.

Crowley stood before the billowing black mist, his silhouette small against it.

"Shh—can you hear that?"

Herbert closed his eyes.
All he heard was the sound of a silent, rolling ocean; the world seemed to drift away.

Crowley was thrilled.
"This is poetry," he said.
"The most mysterious, profound poetry in the universe."

Years later, Herbert's memories of Crowley had mostly faded—
how they worked together, argued, and even how he "accidentally" killed him.

But he would always remember that smile beneath the black mist.
In that moment, he saw the poetry.

wheel-0086.name: Mind Barrier
wheel-0086.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: After Exalt, gain Shield equal to [StateArg1]% of the wielder's DEF.
wheel-0086.lore:
At Mythag, the person you must not provoke is none other than Ms. Sylvia, assuming Doll ranks second.

As the only psychologist at Mythag, this mysterious woman not only comforts investigators traumatized by missions but also calms the turbulent emotions of Awakeners. She does not fear Awakeners; instead, she confronts them mentally—what a formidable figure.

Driven by curiosity and lacking proper respect, some freshmen sneaked into Ms. Sylvia's clinic late at night.

Then, they lost consciousness. The next morning, they woke up in the campus square with throbbing headaches but an unprecedented clarity of mind.

Someone picked up a note that had fallen from their pockets:

"If you need psychological counseling, please make an appointment with Ms. Erica in advance—
Sylvia"

wheel-0087.name: Critical Point
wheel-0087.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: After dealing Damage, the wielder's Crit. Rate + [StateArg1]%. After a critical hit, the Crit. Rate gained from this effect is reset.
wheel-0087.lore:
The noblewoman, with a mix of pity and disgust, covered her nose with a handkerchief and had her servant hand her the unwanted jewelry.
She thought:
That's great—the children in the workhouse can have meat for once.

After sharing all her food with the children and fainting from hunger in the corridor, she said:
I'm fine. Don't call the doctor; the fee would buy several loaves of bread.

When the kind-hearted artist visited with a doctor he knew and offered a long-term arrangement, she thought:
They must try it on me first. I won't let the children receive their so-called treatment until I'm sure it's safe.

Sarah could bear any hardship herself.
But she could not bear that everything she did eventually led to tragedy.

wheel-0088.name: Nobleman's Staff
wheel-0088.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: Increases the base Exalt DMG of the wielder by [StateArg1]%, and increases shield and HP Recovery by [StateArg2]%.
wheel-0088.lore:
There are many speculations circulating in the Mythag campus about Lord Dexter's staff.

"There's no doubt that the lord's staff hides as many secrets as his smile.
Is it inlaid with silver capable of harnessing the power of the Awakeners, or is it secretly filled with gunpowder, ready to transform into a firearm?
Let Lord Dexter reveal it for us!"

After quickly noting down the opening remarks, Hannah aimed her camera at Lord Dexter, eagerly awaiting the words from the holder himself.

"This? Nothing special, just the daily staff custom-made for the Dexter family.
The gem used on mine is even less precious than my brother's.
If I had to say what makes it special...
It has once been stained with my parents' and brother's blood; does that count?"

wheel-0089.name: Data is Flesh
wheel-0089.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: Increase the Base DMG of the wielder's Command Card by [StateArg1]%, and increase Shield and HP Recovery by [StateArg2]%.
wheel-0089.lore:
A story circulates in the underground world of Londinium.

At the busy docks, in secluded alleyways, and noisy taverns...
You never know when or where you might find that girl with a camera, wearing glasses, and dressed in uniform. But as long as you find her, you can buy any information you want, and the only cost is one—

To provide a secret known only to you.

Some traded a drunken truth they overheard at the bar for a deadly weakness of a competitor.
Some exchanged their own weakness for the untold past of a lover.
The girl possesses countless bits of information, but there is one that cannot be traded for any secret.

"About me? Sorry, that's not for sale."

wheel-0090.name: Moment of Reunion
wheel-0090.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: Each time a card of the wielder is played, gain [StateArg1] Keyflare. This effect can trigger up to 3 times per turn.
wheel-0090.lore:
Since being taken in by Mr. Ward Philips, Alistair has accompanied him to numerous high-level meetings of Mythag.


Though termed high-level, it feels more like a monthly debate competition:

Mr. Philips speaks gently and leads the discussion;

Khlarcus is sharp-tongued and unyielding.

They take turns as the affirmative or negative side, arguing endlessly about the future of Mythag or the resource allocation for the Awakening Studies Department next month.

Miss Tawil is a silent observer, never easily offering her suggestions.

He himself strives to be an impartial scorekeeper, occasionally doubling as a referee, and even had to use some combat skills once to prevent the small conference table from facing mishap.


Now, only two members remain in the meeting: one can never leave the Black Pool, and the other can no longer leave the underground in pursuit of that possible "future."

As for that table, it has been lost since that incident.

wheel-0091.name: The Land of Nonexistence
wheel-0091.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: At the start of exploration, add one "Defense" of the wielder to the deck, increasing the Shield of the wielder's "Defense" by [StateArg1]%.
wheel-0091.lore:
Tawil sleeps at the bottom of the Black Pool.

There, it is eternally silent and dark, filled only with a despairing blackness.

No guests have ever visited here, and no sounds have ever reached here.

This is a place where Destruction cannot reach—

Because disaster have already arrived.

As the master of time and sequences, she has witnessed far too many painful ends.

As a part of the rules, she is powerless to stop the night from falling.

In this case, she can only lie dormant in the realm of non-existence, awaiting the arrival of the one who can be called a "variable."

That person will undoubtedly come.

wheel-0092.name: Hand of Oblivion
wheel-0092.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: At the start of exploration, add one "Strike" of the wielder to the deck, increasing the Base DMG of the wielder's "Strike" by [StateArg1]%.
wheel-0092.lore:
A hand gropes in the dark, searching for its master.

On that bloody night, it was cruelly torn from its owner.
Now, the culprit flows around it, emitting a peculiar hum.

"Give up and come with us. The Wax Sculptor will forget you entirely."

The hand remains unmoved, flexing its proud, slender fingers.

"Master will not forget me.
I am his only means of artistic expression—
You don't understand what I mean to him."

"Just as one may forget a lover, forget time, but never forget oneself."

A sympathetic laugh echoes in the darkness.
"Quite the contrary, the first thing one forgets is oneself.
But that's alright—"

Time will explain everything, and the hand will eventually learn:
Nothing is beyond forgetting.
They have flowed for centuries, witnessing human stories unfold, only to be forgotten the next day.
And they continue to flow.

wheel-0093.name: 35mm Magic
wheel-0093.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: The Shields and {Poison} caused by the wielder increase by [StateArg1]%. Each time {Death Resistance} is triggered, all Awakeners gain [StateArg2]% Temporary Crit. Rate and Crit. DMG.
wheel-0093.lore:
Francis once told Lily that stories hold magic.

Back then, Francis would sit by the window with a storybook, her lab coat warmed by the early summer sun, giving off a soft, comforting scent.

Francis spoke in a gentle, unhurried voice, but the long night of surgery had drained her. Before the story could end, she drifted into sleep.

Lily never held it against her mother.

She felt deeply reassured watching her rest against the windowsill, her face tired yet peaceful.


Now, film carries stories and magic where books once did, just as new reels replace old ones damaged by children's mishandling, and the laughter of new friends replaces those quiet afternoons, warm yet tinged with ache.

Yet those buried memories still glow, like exposed film hidden in some forgotten drawer—unseen, but never dim.

wheel-0094.name: The Gaze of Isarawu
wheel-0094.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: Increases the wielder's Shield and {Counter} by [StateArg1]%. When attacked, there is a [StateArg2]% chance for all Awakeners to gain 1 Aliemus.
wheel-0094.lore:
The first Queen's Consort came from Isarawu.
He had a pair of unique Isarawuan eyes, a faint golden hue that shimmered with a weak light in the pitch-black night sky.
But that light faded with the fall of Isarawu, and Wanda retrieved the last vessel from the dark mire, carefully keeping it as a treasured possession.
She met the second and third Queen's Consorts in the Fran Highlands.
They were a pair of greedy twin lords, and Wanda had hoped to use their strength to rebuild the lost City in the Lake.
She was deceived, and thus, the Spine Needles were deeply embedded in their marrow. In their Paralysis, Wanda extracted those two pairs of despicable and rude eyes, leaving them behind as traces of her presence.
The fourth Queen's Consort, Wanda remembered him as "John."
He was a dull mute, but served her well enough. What pleased her more was that "John" also had a pair of faint golden eyes, almost identical to hers. 

 "Let's gouge out the Queen's Consort's eyes."
"As you wish... but did the Queen's Consort do something wrong?"
Wanda swirled the murky water in her cup, feeling the Toxin linger on her tongue. What a fool, even poisoning was done so clumsily.
Watching "John" covered in wounds from Dissolution, she recalled the pairs of lifeless eyes that had seen her off from her homeland while she was still far away among the stars.
They were just like "John's" eyes at that moment.
"...let's say he lacks a sense of humor."

wheel-0095.name: Man in the Mirror
wheel-0095.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: At turn end, the wielder gains [StateArg1] Aliemus. When taking DMG, gain Shield equal to [StateArg2]% of the DMG at the start of next turn. If the current Realm is {Caro}, gain {Crimson Furnace} equal to [StateArg2]% of the DMG instead.
wheel-0095.lore:
The mysterious lady approached her after the funeral.

"Poor child," she said, "you shouldn't have had to go through this."

"Don't you know? After he caused Millie's death, Thompson went after him.

That scum Edward should have died last year, but someone saved him."

"Why? Oh, how should I know? Maybe they're two of a kind, or maybe... the 'saint' himself didn't realize his own sin.
What was his line again? Ah yes, forgiveness. Forgiveness for sinners, for the crimes they commit."

"He believes everyone deserves a second chance. What do you think?
Should we give Edward another chance, after he took Millie, your sister, and your mother?"

"Exactly. People like him never change. So, you know what to do, right?"

She was right. Such people never change.
To end these people, to end this evil, this tragedy, the only way is...

To expose their desires, lay bare their ugliness, let them tear each other apart, purge them completely—
and welcome a land without sin.

wheel-0096.name: Silver Cod's Determination
wheel-0096.descriptionTemplate:
Team Unique: The wielder enters the Silver Cod persona after exploration begins. Switch between the Silver Cod and Cat personas after using Exalt.
Silver Cod: Gain [StateArg1] Keyflare after dealing Damage. Cat: Gain a Temporary Crit. DMG increase of +[StateArg2]% after dealing Damage. These effects share a maximum of 5 triggers per turn.
wheel-0096.lore:
<Bold:Silver Cod 24>
Every time I pass that fish and chips restaurant, I can't help but want to scream.
Why is it always the silver cod that suffers? We're not even real cod!
Is it our fault that our flesh is firm and delicious?

<Bold:Cat 24>
Slurp slurp... meow, meow (indeed, indeed)!

<Bold:Silver Cod 24>
In the Leonian Kingdom, a silver cod dies every minute to become a human meal.
Yes, as we speak, 10 silver cod have already perished.
Ten in total! Father above, do you hear their cries?

<Bold:Cat 24>
Uh... meow? Meow meow, meow meow (where is it)?

<Bold:Silver Cod 24>
It's just a metaphor, silly. You may be a bit dim, but you have a heart of gold, overcoming the base instincts and shortsightedness of a cat.
Listen, I have a plan. I need your help to make it happen...

—Excerpt from "Explosion at Big John's Fish and Chips: Case Summary Testimony B-03." Due to overcrowding at Bethlehem Mental Hospital, the suspect was rejected and held in Londinium Tower for 23 days before being released.

wheel-0097.name: Til the Snow Melts
wheel-0097.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: The wielder's Base DMG +[StateArg1]%. At the start of the battle, the wielder gains 25 stacks of "Snow". Playing a card grants 2 Aliemus and removes 1 stack. Once all "Snow" stacks are removed, the wielder gains [StateArg2]% Crit. Rate.
wheel-0097.lore:
She stood atop the peak, raising the black spike in her hand.

In the raging blizzard, she faced the mountain of madness—and the fate that had abandoned, betrayed, and crushed her.

She would win in the end;
the snow would melt, and she would return in a new form.

That was what she had said.

Elworth is a land of extreme cold, where the snow never recedes.

This is common knowledge.

Just as no warrior, however strong, could challenge a transcendent being, just as no man could overcome their own fate.

And yet, the snow on that mountain melted anyway. Didn't it?

wheel-0098.name: Deep Sea's Call
wheel-0098.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: After the battle starts, gain {STR} equal to [StateArg1]% of the wielder's ATK. If the current realm is {Aequor}, also increase {Tentacle DMG} by [StateArg2]% of the wielder's ATK.
wheel-0098.lore:
In His dream on the throne, the King heard the divine revelation:

The storms of Lemuria will subside.
The abyss of Lemuria will vanish.

With dreams as his hall and stars as his lights,
the King shall shield his kind people, and bring forth the Divine Realm.

O follower of our lord, open your heart to our lord.

In the distant sunless abyss, the great ancient soul slumbers, heeding the whispers of deities.

His will weaves like a web of stars;
in the eternal void, He waits for you to enter His dream.

The storm lies low, the waters grow still;
Earthquakes cease, the land rests in peace.

Calamities dissolve into nothing within His serene dream,
and His people remain safe beneath His protection.

May our song traverse the deep sea,
may He grant us the oracle of a world without calamity,
may He guide us towards endless peace and bliss.

Our faith abides with Him, till the stars decay, till time meets its end.

wheel-0099.name: Kiss of Farewell
wheel-0099.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: After the wielder releases Exalt, they gain {STR} equal to [StateArg1]% of the wielder's ATK. If the current Realm is {Caro}, after releasing Exalt, {Embryo Fusion} +[StateArg2].
wheel-0099.lore:
The girl, reborn from black blood, lifted the sacred blood seed of the Mother Tree amid the desert's thousand-year sandstorms.

"As long as this seed survives in the world, the tree's bloodline will not end."

Then a thousand years passed.

This seed traveled with the tree's descendants, witnessing glory and ruin, displacement and loss, dying and rising again.

Finally, as the mighty river turned into thick black blood, it completed its final inheritance.

It was a fiery and tangled kiss, filled with unfulfilled desires and unspoken parting sorrows, enveloping the new heir together with the warm heartbeat.

That agate-like color, that jade-like sheen, will outlast the black end, pulsing with life.

Then, the first seed of the new world will rise from the ground under her blessing, growing into a colossal tree that covers the sky.

wheel-0100.name: Poetic Bygone Days
wheel-0100.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: At the start of battle, draw 1 Command Card of the wielder and have a [StateArg1]% chance to reduce its Arithmetica Cost by 1. Each time {Death Resistance} is triggered, this effect is repeated once.
wheel-0100.lore:
Since her poems became popular in Cité, her friends have been even more diligent in bringing her flowers.
"There's no need for so many flowers, Colette. It's just too wasteful."
Today's roses are piled up with those from yesterday, the day before, and the day before that. The old flowers have even started to rot due to overcrowding.
"How can it be wasteful? We agreed before to exchange my flowers for your poems."
Colette shook her head, looking at her somewhat awkwardly.
Yes, exchanging flowers for poems—that was their agreement. She could sense that something seemed to have changed, but she was unwilling to think further.
She continued to write poetry, day and night. Once, her poems flowed effortlessly from her pen, but now, her writing has become somewhat labored.
The looks on her friends' faces as they bring flowers have started to feel increasingly unfamiliar. As she gazes at the quickly decaying roses filling her room and smells their rotten scent, she genuinely feels regret and melancholy.
But she has never stopped her pen from moving.
"I will use the flowers that grow from this land to buy us clothes and food. One day, I will even buy the house up there!"
"Everyone has something they were born to do. I was born to work for this garden, for that house... and you, you were born to write poetry."
"I believe in you, Horla! From today onwards, I will keep all your poems safe. In the future, I will exchange my flowers for your poems!"
Flowers and poetry are the promises they make to each other.
She will never stop her pen.

wheel-0101.name: Million Loving Gaze
wheel-0101.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: Increases the DMG dealt by the wielder by [StateArg1]% of their Attack. If the current Realm is {Caro}, each time the {Devouring} Effect is triggered, it further increases the DMG dealt by the wielder by 3% of their Attack.
wheel-0101.lore:
It slithers through the vast chaos, only occasionally casting a fleeting glance at the beings crawling in the world.

Those who catch its eye, like Sorel, are offered "Contract terms" that are hard to refuse. 
Those coarse and tedious, it strips of their flesh, leaving behind an eye that can no longer weep.
These eyes are also known as "the favoured ones." 

They flicker in and out in the cave, filled with both hope and despair, awaiting His Summon.
Yet a thousand years pass, ten thousand years pass, the favoured ones remain forgotten in the shadows.

"Then... you must have been left behind for some reason. What is your mission?" 
"We are waiting." 
"Waiting for what?" 
"Just waiting." 
"Will it cause pain?" 
"On the contrary. The love of the adult fills us entirely. Because that's the only thing we can do." 
"Only by understanding this can one become a true believer."

wheel-0102.name: Slumber Beneath the Glacier
wheel-0102.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: The Shield generated by the wielder +[StateArg1]%. After releasing the Posse, all Awakeners gain an additional [StateArg2]% Temporary Crit. Rate and Crit. DMG, but incur a {Sacrifice} of 3% of Max HP. If the current Realm is {Aequor}, releasing the Posse will also increase {Tentacle DMG} by [StateArg3]% of the wielder's Attack.
wheel-0102.lore:
To await "that moment," the girl slept at the bottom of the glacier.

The deep ice of Elworth froze her body, but it failed to seal away her consciousness.

That wisp of consciousness belonged to a child—and a mother.

Within the dark womb of His daughter, the god dreamed an endless dream.

Over those twenty-five years, did she ever think, yearn, or hope for anything?

No one can answer these questions now.

As for the girl herself... will she open her heart to those around her?

Only time and the future will tell.

wheel-0103.name: Fate Roulette
wheel-0103.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: Increases the wielder's Crit. Rate by [StateArg1]%. Gain [StateArg2] Black Sigil upon killing an enemy, up to 3 times during exploration. After 3 triggers, the wielder's Crit. Rate permanently +[StateArg3]% for the rest of the exploration.
wheel-0103.lore:
Everyone wants to defy fate but fears it.
The brave roar for battle, the cowardly whisper in submission, yet only he uncovered the secret to overcoming fate.
He constantly invites fate to gamble, urging it to deal another round.
He never feared fate, believing he would never lose.
He never questioned what was at stake, wagering everything, even his life.
With a roulette gun, he intimidated countless opponents and became Londinium's infamous gambling madman.
How lonely it is without a worthy opponent.
Until he met that man.
He defeated countless mortals and finally took a seat at fate's table.
He quickly lost everything, then regained it all with interest.
Upon being reborn as a beast, he found the gun that had fired its last bullet.
He reloaded it, pulled the trigger on himself once more.
In silence, he grinned.
He hadn't lost yet, believing that one day he would win in the end.
Even if it meant betting everything.

wheel-0104.name: Gateway of Truth
wheel-0104.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: After the start of the battle, gain Keyflare equal to [StateArg1]% of the wielder's Keyflare Regen. The first Posse issuance in each battle repeats this effect.
wheel-0104.lore:
She is the embodiment of wisdom.

She holds all the knowledge in the world—the highest aim of every scholar in Mythag.

Even hearing a single word from her can bring sudden, profound enlightenment.

Yet she calls herself only a gatekeeper.

What kind of gate?

No one has ever seen it, so the scholars call it "Truth."

She is the gatekeeper of truth, the Athena of Mythag.

Tawil never denies this—nor confirms it.

She simply watches—
watching this place and this world with a constant, silent compassion.

If you ever see that gate, you'll find a familiar eye upon it.

Its gaze is calm and eternal.
It pierces through time, through your very soul.

wheel-0105.name: Never-Ending Performance
wheel-0105.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: At turn end, the wielder gains 5 Aliemus. After the wielder releases Exalt, the next Command Card played by the wielder this turn increases its Crit. Rate, Crit. DMG, Shield, and health recovery by [StateArg1]%.
wheel-0105.lore:
"Not perfect enough, practice ten more times tonight. Your talent is mediocre; if you want to enter the orchestra, you must work harder, understand?"
"Yes, father."
What kind of performance can be called perfect?
His hands flew around without stopping, allowing no mistakes.
He chastised his distracting thoughts, despising his mediocrity.
Countless notes surrounded him; day after day, everything in his life revolved around music.
Fortunately, he was getting closer to a perfect performance each day, gazing at the light at the end of the road in his dreams, yet feeling as if he could never reach it.
The craving for perfection had engraved itself into his soul, yet not everyone is willing to feel this way.
"Presenting the most perfect music for His Majesty and the world, isn't that the duty of the orchestra?"
He looked at the faces that concealed dissatisfaction, as if they were scornful of his former self.
Father, the court orchestra is nothing more than that.
He cast aside the once-revered goal and stubbornly continued down the path of pursuing perfection.
He could not stop his hands to reach out to them, just as he could not halt the hand of his former self.
Each day, he was playing continuously.
Music is his life.
Aside from music, he had nothing.

wheel-0106.name: Shepherd's Staff
wheel-0106.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: At the start of the battle, the equipped gains Aliemus equal to [StateArg1]% of the wielder Aliemus Regen. After the equipped releases Exalt for the first time in each battle, this effect repeats once.
wheel-0106.lore:
Vast flocks of sheep wander over vast grasslands.
There are many, many sheep in this world, and sheep are born to be led by a shepherd.
She has always been the prophet of this world, holding the staff of Knowledge.
She has always been a good shepherd, determined to lead the flock on the right path.
She disciplines the lambs with that staff, drives away the wolves with that staff.
She raises the shepherd's staff high, for she can always foresee the road ahead.
Once she led the flock to abundant water, to fresh pasture.
Now she sees the truth of the world, so she leads the flock into the ultimate embrace.
This way; don't look back.
She smiles, waving her staff of wisdom.
So the sheep cannot see the cliff ahead.
One by one.
The sheep fall, the shepherd sings.
Follow the selfless shepherd, for she has shown you the truest path.
Do not fear, even though you walk through the valley of the shadow of death, her staff is still with you.
Until you both step into the Abyss of annihilation.

wheel-0107.name: Blue Stone Throne
wheel-0107.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: At the start of the battle, grant the "Immortal Dominion" effect to all Command Cards in the Draw Pile with Arithmetica consumption greater than or equal to 3. This card has the {Retain} effect. After being played, all Awakeners temporarily increase their Crit. Rate by [StateArg1]% and remove "Immortal Dominion." If the current Realm is {Aequor}, gain 1 Arithmetica after "Immortal Dominion" is removed.
wheel-0107.lore:
In the endless abyss, the Azure Throne stands sentinel,
The sky hangs low, the stars tremble, and all creation is silent.

Ancient and mysterious entities have been utterly annihilated,
Yet across generations, mortals chant their hymns in dreams.
The abyssal waters and secrets long sealed in dust,
Your whispers surge like a tide, drowning the ravines of the soul.

In that boundless dreamscape, the Prophet and the Madman walk together,
In pursuit of the intangible spirit, fear and reverence intertwined.
The path entangled with dark algae, like a destiny being spun,
Gradually drawing the hearts of the lost toward that Azure Throne.
Ah, the universe's absurdity and its truth,
Where dream and reality intersect, an eternal brilliance flickers.

wheel-0109.name: Macabre Delicacies
wheel-0109.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: The wielder's Base DMG +[StateArg1]%. Whenever the wielder kills an enemy, accumulate {Crimson Furnace} equal to [StateArg2]% of the wielder's ATK if the current Realm is {Caro}.
wheel-0109.lore:
Only the quietly dying are worth savoring.
The living struggle, and struggle makes the taste bitter.

When the platter arrives, the heart at its center is still faintly warm, its surface rising slightly as if mid-beat.
Two eyes gaze up from the side, their pupils boiled docile—milky white, preserving a final instant of shock.

He lifts the knife, then pauses.

The soul inside still screams.
A poet's heart, steeped in sickness and relief, yet tangled with one last regret.
He sets the blade down.
Folds his hands.
Listens.

To truly honor this dish, he must wait.

Wait for the obsession in the meat to unravel.
Wait for the poet's last memories to soften into aroma—ink and herbs, a lover's trembling breath, the shyness of dawn.

He inhales deeply.
The air hums with stories.

Under the whispering tree, he sits, patient as a grave.
The fruit will fall when it is ripe.

wheel-0110.name: Witch's Wide-brimmed Hat
wheel-0110.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: The Base DMG dealt by the first Command Card used by the wielder each turn +[StateArg1]%. If they enter the {Ultra Space}, the wielder gains [StateArg2] Aliemus.
wheel-0110.lore:
