The connection between the witch and the wide-brimmed hat is sometimes like the relationship between the chicken and the egg; it's hard to distinguish whether the hat made the witch or the witch made the hat.
But anyway, Daffodil possesses such an elegant wide-brimmed hat.
This hat originally belonged to the former shopkeeper, back when Daffodil's name did not yet belong to her. She was merely a confused ghost, subdued and bewildered, like an ignorant beast.
The woman who always smiled took her, patiently teaching her, as one would teach a child, little by little, how to be a "person," and the secrets to interacting with others.
The bustling port city gathered the richest crystallizations of human culture at that time. The street where the exchange shop is located featured a plethora of shops, covering a vast array of items, and the shopkeeper always seemed to chat with all the shop owners as if they were old friends.
"She's really amazing," Daffodil thought back then, "but it's not strange; no one could dislike her."
The shopkeeper enjoyed shopping, so for the first time in millennia, Daffodil, who had always played the part of a human whose head she had severed, was asked to give her opinion.
"Which one do you think looks better? This little top hat or this wide-brimmed hat?"
The woman tried on an array of dazzling hats in front of the mirror. To Daffodil, they were merely props to wrap her body and make herself look more human.
But that day, as she watched the shopkeeper wearing the wide-brimmed hat like a giant flower on her head, an image suddenly emerged in her mind of the woman wearing such a hat, and the two of them strolling on the beach.
"This one," she suddenly said, "just this one."
The shopkeeper turned back in surprise, as if she hadn't expected Daffodil to express her preferences so clearly.
"Alright, it is indeed a beautiful hat. Since you chose it for me, I will cherish it well."
Whether it was to encourage Daffodil or not, this hat, just as the shopkeeper promised, became a permanent adornment on her head until the final moment when it was handed over to Daffodil by the shopkeeper, along with her smiling head.
"What a lovely hat, I will definitely cherish it well."
The gorgeous purple-black flowers finally bloomed on the new owner's head.

wheel-0111.name: Blue-Ringed Toxin
wheel-0111.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: The wielder's Keyflare Regen, Exalt Final DMG & Shield +[StateArg1]%.
wheel-0111.lore:
Many years later, facing the prostrate seafarers in prayer, Murphy: Fauxborn would recall that distant afternoon when "Mother" told her the story of the blue-ringed octopus.

The relic in the jar was soft and dazzling, with strange, ethereal blue floating in the water, enticing her young self to draw near involuntarily.

Meanwhile, Miryam stroked her head, compelling her to look at the dim corner of the cabin with an undeniable force—there lay the lifeless body of a seafarer, slumped on the ground, presenting a pallid emptiness as if drained of all toxin.

"Remember, the most tranquil and deadly in the ocean is often the most beautiful."

The cold light reflected off the glass jar illuminated her compassionate silhouette, and Murphy: Fauxborn felt a fear wrapped in beauty.


Time passed quietly in rhythm with the stars, and Murphy: Fauxborn remained as willful as ever, yet she learned to be silent and observant.

"Lemuria, Lemuria..."

Until, upon the altar, that usually calm and gentle face gradually became tainted by madness, she finally realized that she had never truly known Miryam.

The hand that caressed her head, her gentle mother, could also deliver the harshest punishment to the enemies of the Divine Realm.

At that moment, the fear sealed deep within her memory surged forth, ultimately crystallizing into a cold realization—
her kindness was merely a guise of the tentacle, and the beautiful blue ring transformed from a gentle exterior was the very toxin that could seal one's fate with blood.


To this day, the fleeting deity crowned Murphy: Fauxborn.

She looked down at the crawling seafarers, meeting the glimmer of absurd hope in Miryam's gaze, as if she once again saw the small, trembling self that had stared at the beautiful creature before the glass jar.

wheel-0112.name: Lone Feather
wheel-0112.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: The wielder's Shield Generation +[StateArg1]%. Gain Shield equal to [StateArg2]% of the wielder's DEF at turn end, which +2% of the wielder's DEF per turn.
wheel-0112.lore:
Since their birth, the twins have not been allowed to freely fly with their wings.

The second pair of arms growing on their backs resembles two ceremonial vessels displaying the majesty of the Son, always mandated to maintain a certain posture.

In childhood, Pollux was the most protective of those wings among the two, daily grooming and applying essential oils to keep those feathers shining like satin.

He always took advantage of brief moments of solitude to secretly flap those newly flourishing wings, wobbling off the ground in Castor's astonishment, reaching out to touch the embellished dome above.

Compared to how Pollux could make his wings dance in various agile poses, Castor often felt that his wings were as clumsy as two wooden planks.

During their secret time after going to bed each day, Castor quietly watched Pollux leap and spin in the dark, imitating flight within the small confines.

"My brother's wings are so beautiful, but as for me, I might never learn to fly," Castor thought.

So it was on the night of their escape that Castor first felt the coolness and ease of the evening breeze lifting his wings.

The more skilled Pollux tightly held onto his clumsily flying brother, laughing joyously in the sky, drawing arcs of dive and spin like a real bird of the sky.

Castor turned his head to see the moonlight gilding Pollux' long and powerful wings, reflecting a dazzling bluish-green halo among the feathers.

"That must be the most beautiful thing in the world; no matter what happens, I will protect Pollux and safeguard those wings."

Soon, the harsh wilderness life left Pollux's wings injured by beasts.

Castor, heartbroken, picked up the fallen feathers of Pollux and tucked them into his bosom, silently vowing that such a thing would not happen again.

But what he did not know was that this solitary feather would later become his most treasured possession, the last relic he would hold onto about those wings.

wheel-0113.name: The Divus' Visage
wheel-0113.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: In the {Ultra Round}: Gain 3 stacks of {Alight}. When the wielder plays a Command Card, consume 1 stack to increase its Final DMG and Shield Generation by [StateArg1]%.
wheel-0113.lore:
"The Divus is the one watched over by the Lightbearer, born beneath His blessing.

"The Divus must spread His will, bestowing light upon the devout and the righteous.

"He must keep the purity of his soul.

"He shall renounce worldly desires, practice devotion, and serve the Light wholeheartedly.

"The Divus must not speak excessively, nor utter falsehood.

"He shall stay far from sins, distance himself from the sinful, and never be polluted by the polluted souls."


From the moment his memory began, these commandments had wrapped around him like chains, lifting him onto a revered pedestal from which he was forbidden to lose composure, to bow his head, or to indulge in a feeling.


They draped him in wide, splendid robes. They surrounded him with countless squires. He played the part of the sacred representative as required, looking down upon the devout believers who knelt to carefully kiss his hem.


The Divus should be this way. No one cared what emotions or preferences lay beneath that proud and unchanging facade. He had worn the haughty smile for so long that he no longer knew what face he wished to wear when he was alone.


"Your Holiness, it is time for the Rite of Benediction." A maid, her head perpetually bowed, arranged the next segment of his immutable life.


He turned. He raised his head. The smile was there at once, a sacred mask he had long since become. Beneath it, all the confusion, the void, the loneliness, were sealed away as if they had never been.

wheel-0114.name: Navigator at Sea
wheel-0114.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: At the start of battle, gain a Shield equal to [StateArg1]% of the wielder's DEF. After triggering {Death Resistance}, trigger this effect again at the start of next turn.
wheel-0114.lore:
Toot—Toot—

As the sound of the horn echoed, the cruise ship safely docked under the guidance of Corposant.

Passengers laughed as they descended the gangway, tipping their hats to thank her, while sailors rushed over to express their praise.

"Well done, Corposant. If it weren't for your early warning, we would have been swallowed by the storm."

"Yeah, thanks to your correct guidance, we didn't get lost in the sea!"

The sailors laughed, patted her shoulder, and gave her a thumbs-up.

In fact, Corposant could never adapt to such close social interactions; her rigid and rational mind had no thoughts beyond "work" and "duty."

So, she could only attribute this to a part of "work" and "duty."

"I was just doing my job. As a helmsman, my duty is to guide you safely home."

A polite but dull response, yet the sailors who knew her well laughed heartily, bidding her farewell, singing sea shanties, and leaving the harbor with arms over each other.

"The Nautilus can finally set sail. Depart, Nautilus."

The singing gradually faded away, while Corposant remained on the shore, preparing for the future voyage.

The sea is unpredictable, no helmsman can guarantee victory every time; she cannot either. But she is willing to spend countless hours to increase even the slightest odds of success.

Checking, patrolling, learning, verifying, it seems that "work" is the only thing left in her life.

Yet she is willing because all of this is for that moment, the successful helmsmanship, leading everyone home safely.

wheel-0115.name: Thirsting Brush
wheel-0115.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: After the wielder unleashes Exalt, they gain 1 stack of {Collaboration}. The next Exalt of other Awakeners this turn can consume {Collaboration}, increasing its Crit. Rate by [StateArg1]%, and the Awakener consuming {Collaboration} gains Aliemus equal to [StateArg1]% the wielder's Aliemus Regen.
wheel-0115.lore:
On the seventh day of his transformation into a ghoul, he moans in agony as his limbs gradually rot.

Every slight movement tugs at his sensitive nerves, erupting in tearing pain...
mixed with horrifying sparks of inspiration.

Yet, the perplexing shadows always loom over this artist—what kind of brush can depict the most extreme reality?


Pickman's weak eyes scan the area, from the stones by the mansion to the branches above the graves, and finally, his gaze locks onto the exposed white bone in his shriveled, rotting right leg.

As if hearing a sacred summons, Pickman extracts the leg bone, repeatedly rubbing away the adhered flesh and blood, turning it into a hard brush.

He sharpens the tip into a menacing fang, but before it can bite into the canvas, it first pierces the owner's bony fingertip.

Pickman, in pain, releases his grip, and the brush escapes his control.

It dances! It leaps! It twirls!

It wildly sweeps across the canvas, carving chaotic lines of madness.

The lines proliferate and grow wildly, dragging out a constantly expanding, strangely shaped dark smear that slowly but decisively gnaws at Pickman's throat.

A momentary ecstasy pierces his remaining heart like a sharp blade—the swollen organ, in its chaotic form, fills him with tremors of fear, yet revives his waning senses.

They directly and sharply signify pain, strangeness, and desire in a disordered manner, erupting with unabashed, shocking power.

In this inversion, Pickman seems to draw closer to the sacred realm of 'beauty'.


Once everything has calmed down, Pickman extends his clawed hand, now corroded, repeatedly stroking the pale brush.

From this still humanoid shell, a more intense and urgent desire spews forth.

He will together with this brush, complete the final stroke of that monumental painting.

wheel-0116.name: Impending Sun
wheel-0116.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: The wielder's Base DMG is boosted by [StateArg1]%. After playing 5 Command Cards from the wielder, the wielder gains [StateArg2]% Temporary Crit. Rate. This effect can only trigger once per turn.
wheel-0116.lore:
The sun emits over 300 sextillion joules of energy every second, a tiny fraction of which travels over 14.7 billion meters through space in the form of light and heat toward Earth.

This energy sustains all life on Earth, allowing all species across its complex, vast, and chaotic three domains to proliferate and evolve.



"This is the truth of the world, unchanging under any circumstances."

He constantly reminded himself of this to maintain his mental stability.

"But why... is it so hot, so bright?"

Because that which lay 14.7 billion meters away was slowly filling his pupils.

Light and heat radiated from his eyes, burning every inch of his noble body.

"I understand now, I understand now, I understand why light takes 8 minutes and 20 seconds to reach Earth."

"Because we cannot afford the cost of facing the light directly."

As he accepted everything, he began to laugh out loud.

"So it turns out I was closer to the Sun than Icarus ever was."

wheel-0117.name: Light of Intellect
wheel-0117.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: After the first Posse release in each turn, there is a [StateArg1]% chance to draw 1 "Strike" of the wielder's.
wheel-0117.lore:
With reason as a guiding light, one can dispel the mist of life.

Once upon a time, Mouchette had heard such words.

Perhaps it was a technician from Thoreau Company casually expressing this sentiment while debugging a mist lamp?
Or maybe it was a quip from a passerby during a visit to the Filingen restaurant?

Mouchette had forgotten; all she could clearly remember was the habitual social smile on her face when she heard those words, and the sarcasm hidden behind that smile.

"Guiding light?
Ha, what an old-fashioned relic.
People use flashlights these days."

She wasn't unaware that it was just a metaphor; she simply scoffed at the idea.

Because one day, she would die.
And after death, what reason, what mist, would remain—she would be completely liberated from this world.

That was a reasonable and inevitable conclusion.
All she needed to do was enjoy the process before this "inevitability" arrived.

But no one had ever told her that "inevitability" could also encounter "unexpectedness."

Similar mist, similar predicaments, yet there were no mist lamps, no investigation teams, none of the familiar things.
Her axe could sever the monsters of the world, but it could not cut through the mist born of her own loneliness and confusion.

Can she go back?
Will she go back?
When can she go back...

One question after another surfaced from her heart, like an invisible deep water, slowly engulfing her soul.
At this moment, the only thought in her mind, the entirety of her thoughts, was—
Maintain reason.

Don't go insane!
Don't go insane!
Don't go insane!

Whether facing the mist of that world, the mist of this world, or the mist within her heart.

For the sake of returning once more.

wheel-0118.name: Magnolia's Lure
wheel-0118.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: The Fixed {Poison} & {Poison} Trigger effects caused by the wielder +[StateArg1]%. The wielder gains [StateArg2] Aliemus the first time they trigger {Resonance} each turn.
wheel-0118.lore:
That was the second year after my father and I had moved to Shanghai. Evelyne's tearful complaints in her letter left me distraught, and I had already resolved to part ways with her.

Shanghai is a land draped in silk and ebony, where every vulture has sensed the chaos stirring in its slumber. Here, paper notes trade for their weight in gold, and whoever commands the shipping routes can move mountains of wealth at a pittance.

Yet Evelyne—perhaps driven by the neurosis of her obscure and tainted lineage—clung to her so-called ancestral message. She believed that in the East, sinister deities are worshipped by Their offshoots; that like deep-sea fish, They dangle swaying, delicious bait to lure prey into a slow, imperceptible feast.

She was wrong. Shanghai bears not the faintest tang of the brine. There is only the pervasive scent of white magnolia—so cloyingly sweet it drowns the senses in equal parts rapture and terror. The very magnolias that ensnared both my father and me.

...Then, I saw her. The instant the woman named "Xu" appeared, I knew I could never tear myself away from this ancient soil.

She made me want to pull her close; she made me want to make her mine; she made me want to @3. She made made MADE Me mE want WANT wAnTwantWANT to SLICE HER, CUT HER, HACK HER forever forEVERFOREVERforever... @2@3@2!!

Ah... Why am I here? What am I supposed to take away? Who... am I? It no longer matters. She invited me, and now I sit at the table in that grand mansion.

My father—as mad now as I—wears a docile smile. A dazzling array of exquisite side dishes fills the table, though the lidded metal tray seems... out of place.

Xu sits in the sunset, a golden halo rimming her silhouette against the dying light. Slowly, she lowers her black fan. 

She looks like Evelyne, like my childhood playmate, like a fleeting lover from some hazy romance... like my mother. Her face is as serene and exquisitely sculpted as a gilded temple idol.

I hear no slurping. I smell no copper tang of blood from my father. I only stare, entranced, at that face—then at the pale, slender hand that reaches out, lifts the lid, and selects a choice piece of meat. With two thin wooden sticks, she pinches it up, raising it toward me.

She calls to me. The rendered animal fat drips onto the corner of my mouth. "Young sir," she says, "you should eat something."

Obedient and numb, I open my mouth. The magnolia is already in bloom; it rustles softly, swaying down toward me. 

—<Italic:Journal of the Nameless>.

wheel-0119.name: Imprisoned Madness
wheel-0119.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: When switching to the {Raging Waves} stance, gain Temporary Realm Mastery equal to [StateArg1]% of your current Realm Mastery. 3-turn cooldown.
wheel-0119.lore:
Eat eat eat eat...
Delusions born of bewitchment.
Crush crush crush crush...
Incoherent murmurs of the abyss.
Destroy! Destroy!! DESTROY!!! DESTROY!!!!
Unstoppable, unbridled madness.
"Vortice hungry."
The girl gorged on chunks of flesh still dripping with blood. No matter how much she ate, the emptiness in her stomach refused to fade.
"Vortice thirsty."
The girl gulped the rank, rotting seawater. No matter how much she drank, the parched burning in her throat would not relent.
"Vortice sleepy."
The girl hit herself to stay awake. No matter how long she slept, the exhaustion in her mind never lifted.
For only pure destruction can appease the Thing within.
"But... Vortice says NO!"
The girl defied the siren call of fate. She bound herself in specialized steel—a cage she can never shed.
She forged herself into an unbreakable prison; she used her own existence as the shackles to imprison the madness from the deep.
"Vortice, lock the bad thing!"
And so, the great whirlpool Moskstraumen, which appeared in AF 1■■ and claimed countless lives, fell into absolute silence at the end of AF 31■.

wheel-0120.name: Spring in Yakutsk
wheel-0120.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: At turn end, the wielder gains [StateArg1] Aliemus and Keyflare equal to their Keyflare Regen.
wheel-0120.lore:
After the Cataclysm vanished without a trace and the crisis was temporarily resolved, the Mythag Special Operations Team departed from Yakutsk with an "unexpected gain."

Miss Dexter glanced back at the massive pit at the explosion site, noticing that unknown plant sprouts had quietly broken through the soil without her realizing it.

She sighed in relief, feeling lighthearted.

This forest will surely welcome its spring again next year.

The willows will bud when the next spring arrives, and the lilies of the valley will bloom quietly after the next rainy season, with all life embracing new beginnings.

And their journey against the unknown will embark on a new starting point.

wheel-0121.name: Sunfall
wheel-0121.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: The wielder's Base DMG, {Poison}, and {Counter} +[StateArg1]%. Crit. Rate and Crit. DMG +[StateArg2]%.
wheel-0121.lore:
A.F. 314, on a deep winter night, the sound of an explosion echoed through the Akutlin.

The earth trembled, and the woods were illuminated as if it were midday.
Accompanied by the shrieks of life and the wails of nature, the village was razed to the ground.

According to Dexter, a pre-college student in the Awaken system who participated in the "Akut disaster Investigation," among the flames shooting into the sky that night, colors of black and silver shone brightly.

wheel-0122.name: Birth of a Soul
wheel-0122.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: The team's Max HP +10%. The Healing and Shield provided by the wielder increase by [StateArg3]%. After playing the wielder's "Strike," restore [StateArg1]% of lost HP. After playing the wielder's "Defense," gain Shield equal to [StateArg2]% of current life. This effect can trigger up to 1 time each per turn.
wheel-0122.lore:
The strings of time snap one by one, and the flowing keys shatter in turn.

Standing beyond reality, They repair the rifts with the fabric of reality itself, so the unending symphony may sound again in the high heavens.

As for where the tears shed upon waking flow, no one knows.

wheel-0123.name: Dusk and Dawn
wheel-0123.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: Gain [StateArg1] Keyflare at turn end. Gain [StateArg2] Keyflare after triggering {Death Resistance}. Using Emergency Gnosis increases Arithmetica cap by 3 and allows you to draw 3 cards per turn.
wheel-0123.lore:
They rowed through the dark river—pale, silent, exhausted.

A hazy mist stretched out its tendrils and pulled them into the darkness, leaving them to wander an endless night.

Then, they saw a beam of light.

It was the dawn light from a distant shore, flickering, yet bright enough to reach their dim souls.

So they followed it, rowing clumsily through the dark, until they reached the break of dawn;

until they stood before you.


Perhaps this is just a fleeting encounter, but they wave and shout with all they have.
Everyone is bathed in the sacred light.
Everyone is smiling.

They say—

May every gathering be cherished like a feast.
May sincere hearts never cease to beat.
May the Silver Key light our path.

wheel-0124.name: Heart of Silver
wheel-0124.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: Upon releasing Posse, gain [StateArg1]% of the wielder's ATK as {STR} and Shield equal to [StateArg2]% of their DEF. Upon using an Emergency Gnosis, all Awakeners gain [StateArg3] Aliemus at turn end.
wheel-0124.lore:
It was a silver-white, clear, and spotless heart.

Made of pure silver, it beat steadily inside its mechanical frame.

It had once been stained by blood, smeared by mud.

But as death's shadow fell, it returned at last to its original form.

wheel-0125.name: Annihilated Rebirth
wheel-0125.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: After the wielder releases Exalt, Exalt Base DMG +[StateArg1]%, Shield and HP Recovery +[StateArg2]%, stacking up to 5 times. After completing exploration, the Affinity gained by the wielder +[StateArg3]%.
wheel-0125.lore:
You step onto a barren, lifeless wasteland.

All traces of humanity have long vanished. Withered souls trudge through ash and smog, barely clinging to breath.

Survival here is no triumph. 
No one knows where the journey ends. They wander, and wander, and wander still, trapped in an eternal loop of desolation.

Until their steps falter, and they collapse onto the scorched earth, eyes turned skyward, hands reaching out to plead:

May my journey's end become a new beginning.

Let these bones be soil, be sustenance, be spring, so that fragile shoots may rise again through dust.

wheel-0126.name: We Will Meet Again
wheel-0126.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: After switching Tentacle Stances, using {Crimson Furnace}, or unleashing {Annihilation}, draw the wielder's Command Card ×1; each effect has a cooldown of 3 turns. After the Exploration, all Awakeners in the team gain +[StateArg1]% Affinity.
wheel-0126.lore:
You and Ramona couldn't promise to meet again.

As the Gate slowly closed, the temporary farewell you'd envisioned in your hearts solidified into an inevitable eternity.

So, when she lightly hooked your little finger in those final, fleeting moments...
it was already an unexpected and fortunate reunion.

"You haven't changed at all," she says.

For these words—for the faint warmth of her fingertip—you would give everything to leap across the galaxy and meet her, even across two worlds.

Next year on this day, you vow with a pinky promise.

wheel-0128.name: Eternal Weave
wheel-0128.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: Shield generated by the wielder +[StateArg1]%, and when the wielder uses {Pursuit}, Temporary Amplification +[StateArg2]%, up to 5 times per turn. Whenever {Dimension Shuttle} is triggered, the wielder gains [StateArg3] Aliemus.
wheel-0128.lore:
Arachne labored under the shadow of a known futility.

Day after day, she turned the spinning wheel, weaving into its vast, timeless web the joys and griefs of humans, the glory and ruin of empires, and the fleeting spark of poetry and love.

She wrought masterfully-precise, restrained, and gentle.

She discarded the "errant" threads and salvaged the "righteous" ones. And so generation after generation blossomed from decay; civilization after civilization rose from ashes to write itself anew.

Yet the joy once kindled by weaving, the weariness worn into her bones, and her every last tenderness-all had long since withered, ground to nothing beneath the ceaseless turning of her labor.

She had severed her own vulnerability. What remained was will alone, lucid and cold-so that every choice she made might rise from duty, and never from weakness.

The true tragedy, however, lay not in the labor itself, but in her own merciless clarity. She knew that no matter how faithfully she mended and wove, the world would eventually succumb to the pull of the silent void.

Civilizations were but sandcastles before an inevitable, encroaching tide.

Her devotion was a grand performance staged for an empty theater; a vigil to which nothing, and no one, would ever answer.

"Fate has long condemned us to eternal solitude-why should we endure so cruel a sentence?"

"Tear it apart, destroy it... and you will escape this toil..."

She lowered her eyes, gently snipping away those wayward thoughts, then bent once more to her work: weaving, sustaining, suffering, enduring...

All futile struggles and all unsolvable fates had long been woven into this web.

And there, at the center of the web, she beheld herself.

wheel-0130.name: Private Afternoon
wheel-0130.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: After exploration starts, shuffle 1 {Private Afternoon} into the Draw Pile. {Private Afternoon}: Consume 1 Arithmetica. Temporary Crit. DMG +[StateArg1]%. {Retain}. {Exhaust}.
wheel-0130.lore:
Morning rides meant trotting with local squires to gauge their stance on the Reform Bill; luncheons required trading flattery with high-society matrons. At dinner, there were hollow, endless pre-meal prayers under silver candelabras, and even after the opera, the theater corridors were a gauntlet of ceaseless social pleasantries.

"Only afternoon tea on the back lawn feels like the only time that's truly mine."
"You are my first guest for afternoon tea. I’ll let you choose the topic then."

Sorel lifted a freshly poured cup of black tea.
"What would you like to know? Count de Crémay’s stance on the Reform Bill? The Beaumont family’s political gambling? Or perhaps a Viscountess’s secrets?"
"None? You only care about the taste of this pink dessert?"
She took a slow sip, her gaze finally settling on you.
"Then, by all means, have a taste."
"Some things must be experienced firsthand to be truly understood."
"Wouldn't you agree, my esteemed—Excellency?"

Black mist began to seep from the collar and cuffs of the guest's uniform, churning into a mass of dark chaos.
"Hehehe... do you really think you can escape your fate?"
"The sun is perfect, and I intend to enjoy my rare moment of leisure first," Sorel replied. "If you wish to sample the season’s finest tea and sponge cake, help yourself. Otherwise, come back when my break is over."

wheel-0131.name: Uteri Somnia
wheel-0131.descriptionTemplate:
Hand Limit +2. {(Does not stack with other Wheels.)}
The Final DMG dealt by and Shield gained from the wielder's Exalt, as well as the wielder's Keyflare Regen, each +[StateArg1]%.
When you switch to the {Tranquil Sea} stance, the wielder gains [StateArg2] Aliemus. This effect has a 3-turn cooldown.
When you switch to the {Raging Waves} stance, inflict [StateArg3] stacks of {Birth Ritual} on all enemies. This effect has a 3-turn cooldown.
wheel-0131.lore:
Amidst the wind and rain-swept night, the daughter of the deity sat alone beneath the porthole.

The icy moonlight shone upon the swaddling clothes, where the Divine Fetus, dripping with black viscous fluid, slept peacefully.

She began to sing a lullaby.

<Italic:"Hush now, hush, my seaborne dear.">

<Italic:"The Black Moon holds your cradle near...">

<Italic:"Swallow the stars in your dream's mere.">

The song was also a prayer, and deep-sea fish gathered at its call.

The entire vessel became a gigantic altar, much like the day she gave birth to the Divine Fetus.

She cut open her own chest, removed the dripping flesh and bone, repaying the kindness of her upbringing and the destiny of her race with her life.

Her sacrifice did not earn the freedom she craved; death was not eternal rest, but merely the beginning of tragedy.

Since then, her only peace came in the night.

<Italic:"Hush now, hush, my seaborne dear...">

When this lullaby concludes, the great Eternal Realm will pierce the surface and rise from the turbulent ocean.

Embracing this futile illusion, Murphy gently closed her eyes.

wheel-0132.name: Rota Fortunae
wheel-0132.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: Shield generated by the wielder +[StateArg1]%, and when the wielder uses {Pursuit}, Temporary Amplification +[StateArg2]%, up to 5 times per turn.
wheel-0132.lore:
On the day of her seventh birthday, her father rested a spinning wheel called "Moirae" upon her lap.

It bore the name of three Goddesses of Fate long forsaken by prayer, yet the wheel itself was sleek and new, its copper axle gleaming with a metallic sheen, cold to the touch and heavy with silence.

From that day forth, Arachne took up the wheel of fate-not as a choice, but as a sentence. For her unintended words always came true in time, and she could not turn away from the weight of such a burden.

Day after day, she wove without respite, watching the threads of fate coil silently around the intricate wheel, weaving for the world the tremor of a first kiss, the neighing of warhorses, the first cry of a newborn, and the jagged epitaphs carved into stone.

Among thousands of tangled filaments, she selected the "better" paths. She beckoned the dark tide of plague to ebb; she spared history from the consuming flames of war; she allowed civilizations to rebuild from the ruins...

Yet, to sustain that web stretching across time and space, she also wove the strands leading to madness, betrayal, or eternal night.

"What is truly better?"

The silent wheel offered no answer. It only continued to spin.

Virtue and vice, cradles and coffins-all flickered alternately along the same thread.

Young as she was then, she did not yet know the shape of her own fate, yet at the end of countless threads, she had already glimpsed the world's end.

"If the world cannot escape its ruin..."

"No. I love this world."

Arachne gazed upon the wheel, letting the unspoken thought dissolve into silence.

"Then... what of my own fate?"

She murmured to herself, with only hollow echoes wandering between the four walls.

Countless civilizations rose and crumbled upon the web of fate. Pyramids slumbered beneath the sand; spacecraft streaked across seas of stars; languages changed like the turning of seasons; and the names of gods were interred in the dust of forgotten lexicons...

And she sat there always, turning the wheel in her hands, watching humanity reenact its cycle of hope and devastation.

Until, at last, a gentle voice that transcended the eternal prison pierced the silence of the ages.

"Sister..."

She finally withdrew her gaze from the apocalypse, looking down at Nefraea beside her. In those eyes, there was no judgment, no fear, only complete trust, like a blank sheet of paper awaiting the first stroke of ink.

In that moment, Arachne finally understood: love and duty, hatred and ruin-all were the same in the eyes of fate.

And so she gathered those memories she had once snipped and discarded-the pain, the regret, the loss that should have come but never did-and braided them into a single cord. Then, as the heaviest yet most tender of gifts, she tied it around Nefraea's wrist.

"Our bond?" Arachne smiled. "It is fate."

Arachne no longer needed to see her own fate clearly.

For in the moment she picked up the wheel at age seven, she had already chosen the direction of her future.

And the wheel of fate would go on turning. It would bear witness to the battle she was about to enter-a battle with no final curtain call.

wheel-0136.name: Greed
wheel-0136.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: After each stage starts, gain [StateArg1] Black Sigils. Relic Capacity +2. In {Astral Reign}, this effect changes to "Increases the chance for the wielder's Dimensional Image Relic to appear by 100%."
wheel-0136.lore:
Shh... let it end here, with me.
And do not seek its origins.

wheel-0137.name: Hypnotic Pendulum
wheel-0137.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: Increases the wielder's Keyflare Regen by [StateArg1]%. At the start of the first battle, apply 1 stack of {Weakness} and {Vulnerability} to all enemies.
wheel-0137.lore:
The reborn Clementine is like a naive larva, traveling through the human world, wandering in the prosperous world that does not belong to her race, feeling perplexed and confused.
Until that enlightening voice rings out—
"Excuse me, are you Doctor Charlotte? I came to seek psychological counseling from Doctor Charlotte..."
Clementine finally comes to her senses. She doesn't care who Charlotte is; she is simply curious about the new term that the visitor spoke: "psychological counseling".
But she is a mature hunter and will never reveal her ignorance, so she smiled, as if she were Charlotte, the savior spoken of by the visitor.
Tears, torment, helplessness, no need to provoke; the visitor has already squeezed out the pain from the depths of her soul.
Before leaving, the visitor looked at Clementine with a relaxed expression and sighed, "Thank you for being willing to listen. I feel so much better."
Then came the sound of the door closing, along with Clementine's laughter, as she tasted the sweetness for the first time.
This laughter started softly, like that of a larva's buzzing, and then grew louder, as if it was the screech of an adult insect.
And in this laughter, the insectoid named Clementine swiftly matured.
Everything that followed fell into place. She became a psychologist, carrying a smile and a pendulum, diving deep into each patient's heart.
She continues to walk through the world of humans, still not belonging to this prosperous world, but no longer confused and perplexed.
For the entire human world is her hypnotic hunting ground.

wheel-0165.name: Sakura Reveries
wheel-0165.descriptionTemplate: After the exploration starts, shuffle 1 {Vernal Message} into the deck: Arithmetica Cost 1, gain [StateArg1] Keyflare, permanently transforms into {Sakura Blossoms} after played: Arithmetica Cost 1, gain [StateArg2] Keyflare, after playing it permanently transforms into {Hanafubuki}: Arithmetica Cost 1, gain [StateArg3] Keyflare, permanently transforms into {Ichigo Ichie} after played: Arithmetica Cost 1, choose 1 from 3 random unlocked Posse to immediately unleash and trigger 2 times, permanently transforms into {Vernal Message} after played. All of these Cards have {Retain}.
wheel-0165.lore:
As a top student at Mythag, Ramona operates on pure logic and reason. She is calm, witty, and incredibly reliable.
Yet occasionally, even she falls into quiet reverie when faced with the world’s absolute terror—or its absolute splendor.
Gazing at the endless sea of pink, Ramona fell silent.
She knew these cherry blossoms would fade within three weeks, their lush branches soon turning bare and desolate.
A human life lasts far longer than three weeks or thirty years, yet like these trees, it comes only once.
The greatest human illusion is the belief that one will live forever.
Ramona understands this.
Like the blooming and wilting of flowers, human life and death are ultimately just phenomena of nature.
Ramona understands this, too.
But humans always seek to give their existence meaning; that is why every new student enters the gates of Mythag.
They are the pioneers against Dissolution, the final line of defense for humanity’s future.
Their lives aren't just natural phenomena; even destined to perish, they are actively shaping the course of this world.
They fight side by side, weathering every storm together.
Like two blossoms on the same branch, they bud together, sway in the wind together, and wither together.
Now, it is time to go back.
The one who relies on her—and whom she can rely on in return—is still waiting.

wheel-0166.name: To the Stars
wheel-0166.descriptionTemplate:
Team Unique: For every 1% of Sigil Yield the wielder, their WoDs, and Covenants have, recover [StateArg1]% Max HP at the end of battle. This effect diminishes by 30% at the end of every turn, and is calculated individually for each battle.
After choosing {Perception} at {Ashen Ruins}, refreshes will be free at this {Ashen Ruins}.
wheel-0166.lore:
You wander among the nebulae, witnessing the stars fall, one by one.

Burning stars. Shining stars. Freezing stars. Dimming stars. Before you can bid them farewell, they drag their long tails across the vault of heaven and fade away.
You cup what remains in your hands. You want to etch every trace of their existence somewhere deep enough to keep.


But it sifts through your fingers, every image and afterimage, through the gaps of thinning memories, out into the boundless darkness of time and space, between one moment and the next.

It turns out you, too, are merely one falling star among ten thousand.


But you do not stop.

You will press on, through the long silence after ending, toward the dawn of a new age.

Until the stars fill the sky once more.

wheel-0167.name: Fragrant Morphogenesis
wheel-0167.descriptionTemplate:
Arithmetica Limit +1, Effects of the same type cannot stack.
For every 10 Base Aliemus the wielder has, they inflict +[StateArg1]% {Corrosion} stacks and cause +[StateArg2]% {Embryo Fusion}.
wheel-0167.lore:
A dandelion, smaller than a bird, lighter than a breeze.

When the dandelion passed through the first wasteland, it asked the tumbleweeds scattered about: How do you reproduce?
The tumbleweeds said: "We let our seeds drift in all directions until they find soil where they can take root."
Wandering travelers have no home, the dandelion sighed as it caught another gust of wind and flew away.

When the dandelion passed through the second desert, it asked the sparsely scattered cacti: How do you survive?
The cacti said: "We bury our roots deep underground to draw from precious water sources."
Living tenaciously even in the harshest environments, the dandelion pondered as it departed.

Before long, the dandelion passed over a city.
The city lights emerging in the dark night were the marks of human life.
At the end of its long journey, the dandelion's seeds were finally about to drift down upon that land.

wheel-0168.name: Muddied Fairy Tale
wheel-0168.descriptionTemplate: After using {Crimson Furnace}, the wielder gains Aliemus equal to [StateArg1]% of Base Aliemus. 3 turns cooldown.
wheel-0168.lore:
Besides dry research papers, the bookshelf held a few children's books that looked rather charming. <Italic:Cinderella. Snow White. The Little Mermaid. Rapunzel...> Why their owner had kept these here—there was no longer any way of knowing.

Those stories wove a world nothing like reality. "Prince." "Princess." "Love." These words set the young girl's heart aflutter.

Countless lights glimmered across the night sky, spilling down to earth as though gathering into a river of flowing radiance.

In the falling moonlight, the girl quietly rose onto her tiptoes, pretending to step into a pair of glass slippers, transparent as air. As though she had always been waiting—for this deep, lonely, endless night to finally break into dawn..."

wheel-0169.name: Compass to False North
wheel-0169.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: The wielder's {STR▼} effect +[StateArg1]%. After switching to the {Raging Waves} stance, Temporary Crit. DMG increases by an amount equal to [StateArg1]% of the wielder's Base Crit. DMG, with a 3-turn cooldown.
wheel-0169.lore:
The old witch drifted between merchant ships, her figure always cloaked in the shadows of her tattered robe. The compass, she said, would guide people exactly where they were meant to go. The sailors laughed and jeered at her herb-scented nonsense, tossed down five Leonian Florins, and walked away with the exquisite brass trinket.
Later, they docked at a small harbor town—one that would eventually lose its name.
Pontos loved this story. The compass's owner was charmed by Pontos.
After all, no one could dislike Pontos—he was humorous, cheerful, as carefree as a passing breeze.
Gifted to his beloved nephew, it was meant to be a wonderful keepsake, a key to awaken his yearning for the distant town. Yet, it concealed the boy from him. Pontos would not allow it, nor would he ever permit his nephew's life to take any other course.
Years passed, and as Pontos wandered through countless moonlit nights, searching for the figure who should have returned to Ebwynnos, this was exactly what he thought.
Oh right, that futile, protective spell had worn off at last. Pontos could finally bring him back.
Pontos knew the old tale well—that the compass guides people exactly where they are meant to go. What he failed to realize, however, was that just as every ship charts its own course, different travelers will inevitably choose different paths.

wheel-0170.name: Dreaming of Wonderland
wheel-0170.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: After exploration starts, add 1 {Dreaming of Wonderland} to the deck. {Dreaming of Wonderland}: Consume 2 Arithmetica, grant the wielder [StateArg1] Aliemus and 3 stacks of {Mental Interference}. {Retain}. {Exhaust}.
wheel-0170.lore:
Legend has it that if a child chases a wild rabbit through the forest, there is a chance they might fall down a tree hollow into another world.

Padding softly in her bare feet, Lily stepped onto the cobblestone path.
"Lily likes it here... There are no glass shards on the ground, and the air doesn't smell like disinfectant."

Lily took a deep breath of the fresh air. The wind sweeping through the woods carried the exotic scent of flowers.

Above, the interlaced canopy wove a dome like stained glass, while the dappled sunlight spilled across the ground, sparkling like dancing gems. So resplendent and mesmerizing was the scenery that Lily stood spellbound, hardly knowing where to look first.
"Oh, there you are, Mr. Rabbit!"

A pair of familiar long gray ears popped out from the undergrowth, and Lily hurriedly chased after them.

With every step she took, the moss beneath her feet blossomed into tiny silver flowers, but Lily had no time to linger. She had something much more important to say to Mr. Rabbit.

She waded across a brook that rippled with the amber hue of honey, and passed through a thicket of singing reeds.

Panting for breath, Lily finally caught Mr. Rabbit by his pom-pom tail.

"Mr. Rabbit, would you like to be friends with Lisa?"

"Lisa is my friend, but everyone else is very afraid of her now... If it's Mr. Rabbit, I'm sure you could be friends with her!"

The very moment Mr. Rabbit turned around, a myriad of fireflies swirled up into the air.

"Of course, @2 is a friend to all. But now, it's time for you to rest."

And so, cradling Mr. Rabbit, who was now her friend, Lily made her way to the foot of a towering tree deep within the forest.

Wrapped in the warm, drifting glow of the fireflies, Lily leaned against the ancient roots and fell quietly asleep.

"Subject: Lily. Clinical presentation: respiration steady, REM normal. The 359th consciousness interference experiment: successful."

It was a realm of fantasy, as gorgeous and ethereal as a dream.

wheel-0171.name: The Living Cage
wheel-0171.descriptionTemplate: Team Unique: The wielder's {STR▼} effect +[StateArg1]%. For the current exploration, each kill increases the wielder's fixed Aliemus recovery at turn end by 2, stacking up to [StateArg2] times. After switching to the {Raging Waves} stance, Temporary Crit. DMG increases by an amount equal to [StateArg1]% of the wielder's Base Crit. DMG, with a 3-turn cooldown.
wheel-0171.lore:
If wishes could truly come true, Pontos would wish for farewells to announce themselves before they arrived.
His parents vanished without a trace. On a quiet afternoon, his sister Leisha hired a rickshaw, which later brought them to a long iron behemoth reeking of diesel, rattling and roaring, until it spat him out at a seaside town.
Leisha never breathed a single word about their parents' whereabouts. After a fierce argument, he stormed off, rushing into the harbor thick with the stench of brine and fish.
The sea folk, with unnaturally wide-set eyes, were peddling bizarre fish dredged from the abyss—depths no human strength could ever reach.
"Hey, stay back. Those fish might be cursed, just like the creatures that caught them," a passerby warned in a hushed tone.
No one wanted to see this handsome, newly arrived young man get mixed up in trouble.
He offered his habitual easygoing smile, but his gaze was irresistibly drawn to a dark, decaying lure. Right beneath it lay a gaping maw bristling with sharp teeth, ugly and contorted, curving inward in savage disarray. A phantom voice murmured from the depths of his mind, recounting the life of this dead creature.
<Italic:Down in the lightless deep, it lured, plundered, and stopped at nothing. Its body was a fortress, a living cage, trapping its offspring, its lovers, its enemies, and its prey.>
<Italic:All the beautiful things in its life were meant to be fleeting, but it held them all fast. It made its own body their vessel, fusing them in an inseparable, eternal embrace.>
Pontos gave his head a sharp shake, looking around in terror amidst a sharp, ringing noise in his ears.
The voice receded like a tide. He dismissed the whole episode as a fleeting delusion, silently mocking his own frayed nerves.
And so, entirely unaware, he began his descent into his preordained fate.
